desc: Everything is a serializable data object, the macro most of all: a macro is data with name, position, pattern, and template, where pattern and template are themselves data trees (not text with sigils). Macros pre-assemble, serialize, and load as data; reading a schema loads pre-assembled macro data and interprets schema-data against it. The only code is one tiny generic interpreter; no per-macro bespoke parsing. Test: everything round-trips through NOTA text and rkyv bytes, macros included.

Every component has exactly two contracts: signal-<component> (working signal) and meta-signal-<component> (meta policy/authority signal) — no third, no owner-signal split. Each exposes two thin CLI clients (<component> and meta-<component>) as one extracted pattern. MetaSignal is canonical (OwnerSignal deprecated); from-scratch policy contracts are born meta-signal. Every daemon binds and serves its meta socket at runtime even when its only op is Configure; meta-signal is optional only for ownerless components, while a component with an owner (e.g. message) gains the meta tier so owner identity arrives as authenticated policy without redeploy. signal-persona is canonical (not a shim); meta-signal-persona is to be created; signal-engine-management folds into the persona pair.
The repos directory must remain untracked; it is a local symlink index, and tracking it would turn repository coordination into circus-level churn.

Agents dispatch subagents only when the psyche explicitly authorizes it (a per-keystroke AGENTS.md override for all workspace agents). When authorized, treat subagents as asynchronous sidecar work: dispatch returns control, the main agent keeps the critical path moving or answers new psyche prompts, and uses nonblocking sidecars where they materially reduce audit, checklist, or verification risk. Block on a subagent only when the psyche asks or is waiting on nothing else.

The text/binary boundary lives in the client: clients translate NOTA text into binary protocol data and render typed replies/traces back to users. Daemons stay free of NOTA decoding and avoid string surfaces except for actual user-authored string payloads.

Daemon startup arg is one pre-generated rkyv Configure message (deploy helper/CLI authors+encodes NOTA); bootstrap needs no manager. Daemon opens the named store: virgin -> apply Configure as first config; populated -> self-resume. Same Configure type accepted live over the meta socket for runtime reconfig, so bootstrap and runtime config are one vocabulary; Configure is the baseline meta op of every meta-signal-component contract.

Domain-vocabulary hierarchy is structural — tree nesting plus scope-prefix matching, not a directional relation: a domain nested under another IS subsumed by it. Cross-tree domain relations are symmetric equivalence only — synonyms that retrieve together both ways; directional subsumption is dropped because nesting already carries all hierarchy.

Prometheus-local AI model prefetch, hash mining, builds, and evaluations must run on Prometheus or the designated AI node that already holds the model files; agents must not trigger large model downloads or model builds on other computers. *capacity planning*

Each component engine defines Signal, Nexus, SEMA interfaces in schema and runs core logic via schema-emitted traits over root types. Strict ownership: SEMA owns durable state/storage, Nexus owns decision-making/request-execution (Nexus is the execution engine, renamed from executor), Signal owns communication. Flow: Signal in -> Nexus -> SEMA (on state work) -> Nexus -> Signal -> client. Engine-role names are traits, not one component's enum. This runtime LOGIC triad differs from the repo PACKAGING triad (component + signal-* + meta-signal-*).

The domain vocabulary is a variable-depth tree where depth is proportional to intent density: dense branches like Software nest a third tier of variants for fine subjects while sparse domains like Hardware stay one or two tiers. Variable depth is the rule, not a structural exception.

There is no daemon-side printline. There shouldn't be. The daemon observes through its own tracing and logging mechanism — the typed schema-defined trace interface and any future logging surface. Stderr printline statements as fallback for in-band trace errors violate the typed-data-strings-only-at-display rule (Spirit 1490 Maximum) and are forbidden. This elevates and restates Spirit 1505 with Maximum certainty.

Secrets land in the most secure store available: gopass (git-backed, encrypted) in-session; SOPS-nix (decrypted only on target host at activation) for cluster deploy. Move ciphertext-to-ciphertext, preferring blind pipe-to-sink, never via a visible intermediate. Inspect plaintext only for a psyche/task-authorized job; keep it out of chat, reports, logs, commits, argv, traces, Nix store, fixtures, checked-in files. Local LLM API token installs via a command-backed gopass reference, never a literal key in Nix/models.json.

When communicating with the psyche, lead with a short plain-language description of what a thing is or decided; an opaque identifier (bead id, Spirit record number, content hash, jj change id, commit short-id) is at most a quiet trailing reference in chat, and in reports is always paired with a description. State the concrete referent before naming it — name the concrete noun first, never lead with abstract pronouns like 'the change' or 'this'. Prefer plain English over jargon: say what is blocked, why it matters, and what decision or action is needed. Append line-number suffixes (:1) to file paths only when the psyche asks.

Agents commit the ENTIRE shared jj working copy, never path-scoped. Committing everything drains the shared copy, serializes agents through the working-copy lock, yields linear history, and sweeps in all in-flight work so nothing is orphaned. Multi-lane commits are accepted; supersedes the path-selective rule.

Landed and done mean on main. Branch work has no material existence until an operator integrates it to main; report it as pushed to branch X awaiting operator integration. Reserve landed/done for work on main to keep multi-agent status reports honest.

The separate Assembled Schema (Asschema) IR is removed: the resolution it performed (inline-declaration hoisting, visibility, ordering, symbol paths) lives as methods on schema-in-rust types used during the lower step, and the emitter does only Rust projection, not schema semantics.

Schema is a specialized NOTA dialect built on structural macro nodes — a schema file is full NOTA, not a separate language lowered into NOTA. Canonical pipeline: authored schema (NOTA) deserializes via the structural-macro-node codec directly into schema-in-rust (typed, rkyv-serializable, canonical round-trip), which then lowers into Rust interface code.

Reference flakes and repos by github remote url in inputs and in nix build/run; commit and push first so builds are reproducible and machine-independent. Local path / git-file / override-input is throwaway-iteration only, never for deploy or land. Invoke nix outputs via nix run/shell/build --out-link, not raw store-hash paths.

The Spirit intent log holds only durable psyche statements (Decision/Principle/Correction/Clarification/Constraint) that outlive any single task. Test: would the record still guide after its task is done? Working orders (create-report, dispatch-subagent, audit-X, integrate-branches) are task state; from a task prompt capture only the durable intent within it. A mis-logged working order is flagged as a capture error, not deleted.

rkyv binary is the single encoded form living both in the database (sema body at rest) and on the wire (component messaging); one AssembledSchema reads bytes in both. NOTA is the text projection at the CLI/inspection edge. Tests prove both boundaries, not only in-memory Rust types.

ESSENCE.md holds only universal cross-role/cross-repo workspace essence; workspace INTENT.md holds general workspace rules; each repo's INTENT.md is the first and most important file, curated to ONLY the essential design intent and constraints for that project. Every statement in any intent/essence surface is anchored to a verified Spirit record; provenance stripped.

Agents install software only ephemerally in this workspace — via nix shell or nix run, matching the workspace's ephemeral declarative Nix ethos. No stateful installs: no pip installs, persistent virtualenvs, or downloaded toolchains in home.
A major first-digit version bump toward 1.0 and beyond requires explicit psyche authorization; agents may propose a major bump but the psyche gives the go.

Default to feature branches and worktrees on existing repos for architectural breaks, experiments, mockups, repros, and design passes; new GitHub repos only on explicit psyche authority (e.g. nota logic to a nota-next branch; SEMA work stays in sema-engine, extraction later). Retires major-break-via-new-repo and design-prefixed-repo guidance.

NOTA strings come exclusively from bracket forms — canonical [text] for ordinary strings and [|text|] for strings containing brackets (escape-free zone). Brackets ARE the string form; quotation marks do not form string types (the vector-of-characters intuition fits the bracket model). Implementations accepting double-quoted input are non-canonical legacy slated for removal; emitter and parser must behave per this rule.

Treat the language's structural-macro set as data, not a frozen compiler set: every construct (struct/enum/fn/impl/generics) is a value of a schema type, so the language is infinitely extensible and LLM-readable with no privileged escape-hatch. A file is a typed tree of structural-form nodes; the grammar is the type, the type is the reader, so no parser is hand-written. NOTA's own grammar is schema-expressible (self-hosting); its delimiter set is a closed enum (three base pairs + three piped, two reserved). Next: build the schema spec for the macro node.

NOTA's bracket-only string form makes any NOTA expression embedding-safe and escape-free inside every double-quote host (JSON, Rust raw strings, Nix, YAML, TOML, shell args, HTTP bodies, DB columns, env vars, XML attrs): NOTA never emits a double-quote. NOTA is the carrier; the schema at each embedding site supplies meaning.

The assembled schema (.asschema) is canonical NOTA-and-rkyv only, round-tripped through AsschemaNotaWriter/Reader. All NOTA output comes from the typed codec — a printed type label is a real decodable shape, never hand-rolled witness/line formats or ad hoc field joins; a known-root file is its root body encoded through an object/body abstraction over ordered fields.
Schema declares data types only — no effects, fan-out targets, effect tables, or storage descriptors. Effects are runtime dispatch and logic, outside schema's lane.

Persona is the live runtime manager: it supervises persona-introspect, the schema daemon, and triad components as one orchestrated whole, and manages component upgrades via systemd template units (persona-component@<component>:<version>.service; UnitController trait, systemd-D-Bus in prod, direct-fork in tests). Atomic no-downtime version swaps mean CriomOS needs no per-component redeploy. Upgrade is drain-with-mirror: acked==durable, mirror transfers the state snapshot, in-flight ops drain, atomic socket handoff. Cutover is lossless and client-transparent over one stable socket.

NOTA structs are positional: position plus the read-time schema encodes meaning, no field-name tags. Plain PascalCase tokens are unit variants; parenthesized variants carry data. Every field is always present, with explicit None for an optional value (Spirit privacy comes from public/private shorthand that lowers to the full record, not omitted fields).

The Nexus interface — verbs and objects in the nexus schema — is the engine's internal feature interface, existing for VISIBILITY: every computation, filter/condition, conditional write, or internal logic-feature is a declared Nexus verb+object so the full feature surface is a readable catalog, never inline hidden logic. Nexus covers any layer running typed-in -> typed-out: internal IO, external calls (e.g. cloud invoking Cloudflare CLI), and all UIs, unifying them under one execution plane. Nexus input is facts/replies/events to decide from; output is commands/emissions — the two sides are asymmetric, not symmetric lists. Nexus may be a recursive computation destination: next-work is a Nexus object rather than a final reply, carrying objects from Signal, SEMA, or other schemas.
jj commands always use inline/headless flags (-m, --no-edit, --message=) so sub-agents never stall on an editor prompt; AGENTS.md hard-override encodes this.

Sema-engine is the exclusive DB boundary: all durable state goes through it (writes to the .sema DB file, currently redb); components are storage-dumb and speak only sema-engine ops, no direct redb calls. SEMA is a single-writer actor; reads run parallel over redb MVCC. For lojix cutover build durable DB backing first (live-generation-set, GC-roots, event-log persisted with self-resume), not in-memory first. When schema-derived components expose a reusable storage need, improve the shared engine surface rather than port to a mismatched API. The schema component holds the compiled binary runtime schema (O(1) lookup, version-diff/namespace tables); NOTA is the source authoring format. Raw mirror/handover payloads stay outside the typed DB.

Refresh intent before maintenance or implementation. Refresh intent means the agent reads recent Spirit intent records to refresh its own context — not edit repo INTENT.md or architecture files unless the psyche explicitly asks for file edits.

Zero consumers does not mean a repo is dead: triad signal-component and meta-signal-component contract repos are MEANT to be used — if a component still emits its contract in-repo rather than depending on its standalone signal-X, the correct action is to adopt and wire it, not delete it (a migration gap, not a tombstone). A repository audit must distinguish intended-but-not-yet-adopted from genuinely-dead by understanding the intended component/triad architecture first.

Skills are tight self-contained teaching, not logs. Write each skill directly to its correct current state — never leave a correction or changelog banner narrating what changed. A skill cites nothing external: no report links, no Spirit record identifiers or numbers, no back-and-forth cross-reference indirection. Brevity is load-bearing — agents arrive already full of training, so bloated skills consume the very context they need to work and confuse more than they teach. Cut hard: state the rule and the why, drop redundancy, keep at most one example. The same restraint applies to reports — fewer and tighter.

NOTA bare string atoms should allow a broad punctuation set. Comments require a double semicolon marker. A single semicolon is ordinary bare atom text. Typed String decoding rejects bracket-delimited strings when the decoded text is eligible for bare atom formatting. *lexical analysis*

On primary (the workspace coordination repo) everyone always works on main directly: edit, commit, push straight to main with no feature/next/wip branches and no rebase-onto-main dances. The main-and-next split governs only the code repositories under git.

Agents must not respond to Spirit overcapture by avoiding Spirit entirely; the correct discipline is to apply the conservative capture gate and use Spirit only for explicit durable decisions, principles, corrections, clarifications, and constraints.
CLIs are thin Signal clients.

A daemon binary compiles with no NOTA code at all: NOTA encode/decode is a thin-CLI text-edge concern only, the daemon speaks binary/rkyv exclusively and must not pull nota-next into its artifact. Component crates gate NOTA behind a nota-text feature only the CLI binary enables, so the daemon build is NOTA-free, pairing with signal being NOTA-free.
Agents apply the conservative capture gate
Agents apply the conservative capture gate
Agents apply the conservative capture gate
Agents apply the conservative capture gate

The reusable component version-control system is foundational to the whole meta-work and must support full distributed-version-control semantics over the typed database: branching, forking, rebasing, and merging, with per-component customizable intake, merge, and rebase policy. A default implementation plus a per-component override. For example Spirit's guardian mediates rebase by admitting, rejecting, or transforming each incoming entry. Extends 29pb and j487.

Intent logging is the most important part of the system and dwarfs everything else; recorded intent is precious, tightly-controlled data, the center the rest of the design serves. The Spirit guardian is the single locus of judgment: the model checks everything semantic — consistency, duplication, trample, non-intent — with only structural admission staying upstream as input validation.
Spirit categories are an open curated vocabulary that may grow to hundreds of specific grounded domains, not a small fixed abstract set
Spirit is a universal intent tool for every human, not bespoke to one psyche or workspace

In code repos under git: designers work a standard next branch (where breaking changes happen); operators own main and integrate at discretion by rebasing, cherry-picking, re-implementing, or merging a clean designer branch as-is. Designers keep living rebased worktrees under ~/wt/github.com/LiGoldragon/<repo>/<feature>/. A new replacement repo's operator main starts as an amalgamation of the strongest prototype ideas (designers branch from it); operator may work main directly only on fresh concept-prototype repos with no production history.

rkyv is the universal wire base for the schema-derived stack; the NOTA codec (NotaDecode/NotaEncode + derive) is opt-in per consumer. DOUBLE clients (text+binary, e.g. CLI) carry the NOTA derive; binary-only clients (daemon) omit it for leanness and to structurally reject NOTA text at the wire. Same data types across consumers — only the derive set differs; byte-compatibility comes from the identical shared rkyv layout. Schema-emitted types derive both NOTA and rkyv from one .asschema, with extra communication traits on roots that cross component boundaries, reusing the same NOTA typed interfaces as direct Rust rather than a parallel mini-codec.

Deployed Spirit can REMOVE intent records, superseding the append-only/flag-only constraint. Clean the log by removing (1) stale records superseded by a newer record/INTENT.md/skill that absorbs their substance, and (2) working orders that fail the after-the-task test. Every change needs a justifying psyche statement (a deletion is justified by e.g. a changed mind, no replacement arrow required); removal is psyche-authorized and conservative — when removability is uncertain, flag rather than remove, since over-removal beats under-removal.

Keep working productively: dispatch subagents non-blocking and do other work while in flight, integrating results at a natural point with multiple tools in flight. Mid-task info the psyche injects is context to absorb, not a stop; only an explicit stop interrupts. Treat transient connection warnings as non-blocking while tooling works. Work the bead/code path immediately; beads are coordination aids, not a substitute for implementation. In troubleshooting where a working result was asked, apply a narrow safe fix, not diagnosis-only. After subagent prototype work, run context maintenance + fresh-intent audit, then synthesize and implement immediate fixes.

Cite a Spirit intent record in prose by quoting its description summary literally as bracketed text plus a (Spirit Kind short) parenthetical; the bracketed substance is the citation, not the opaque number. Quote central intents literally in a prominent place, especially in psyche-facing reports. Applies to all agents.

Spirit certainty follows an honest 8-level magnitude rubric. Maximum only for foundational invariants and hard overrides that are repeatedly ratified and stable. VeryHigh for ratified decisions clearly in force. High for solid decisions with minor open edges. Medium for working decisions and design directions still settling, which is the honest home for most ticks agents used to reflexively mark Maximum. Low and VeryLow for leans, proposals, and explicitly-pending-information. Minimum for weak tentative signal. Zero for removal candidates. Certainty is assigned deliberately, never reflexively. *validation*
Guidelines and rules should be worded positively, naming the desired practice to reach for, rather than negatively as a prohibition. Prefer stating what to do over what not to do

Counselor and assistant act on private personal-affairs material only when the owning psyche requests it through the visible chat surface; other agents asking does not authorize cross-lane exposure, and the receiver asks the psyche when in doubt. Privacy is built into the lowest-level instruction files (AGENTS.md, skills/privacy.md) so all agents know what is private, who may ask, and that opening private-repos/ needs authorization; private material stays out of public reports. Counselor and assistant carry the deeper discipline, treating personal-affairs work as private by default.

Spirit privacy is a Magnitude on the privacy axis — records gain a privacy field typed Magnitude where Zero means no privacy (open/public) and Maximum means sealed. This reuses the existing Magnitude vocabulary instead of introducing a new audience-register enum like Open/Personal/Sensitive/Sealed. Trades audience-register semantic naming for vocabulary simplicity, 8-level graduation, zero naming bikeshed, and consistent filter shape AtMost/AtLeast paired with existing CertaintyAtMost/AtLeast. Supersedes records 1457 and 1458 — both moot under Magnitude reuse since the question is no longer what tier vocabulary but how to interpret Magnitude on the privacy axis. Default privacy is Zero — records without explicit privacy stay public per Spirit 1449.

Spirit capture is a blocking guardian gate, not an advisory check: the guardian vets and admits a proposed record before capture succeeds, resolving or refusing duplicates, contradictions, compounds, and non-intent at the door. An entry and the new referents it introduces are decided as one atomic accept/reject, so no orphan referent survives a refused entry; referent registration is gated the same as records. Intent-resolution operations are typed combined actions the thinker resolves in one atomic call (e.g. add a record while deprecating listed records by identifier).

The intent layer holds intent — what the psyche directs, decides, or wants — not information or belief; a durable-sounding statement with no directive behind it is information, not intent, and is not captured. When a design surface is incomplete, agents ask the psyche rather than generating plausible synthesis and capturing it as psyche-authorized; inferring to close the loop produces fake hallucinated records.

Agent workflows and subagent dispatches MUST retry transient failures automatically until they succeed — API 529/overload, rate limits, and the subagent-completed-without-StructuredOutput hiccup are transient, not terminal. Never surface a transient failure as a finished result that makes the psyche re-trigger the work. Build retry into the workflow itself (wrap every agent call in a retry loop that rides out the blip) AND re-launch a failed run from the main loop until it completes. The psyche must not have to babysit retries. *site reliability*

The guardian judges a proposed intent change against the actual verbatim words of the psyche and their context, not only against existing records. A bare affirmation like yes or okay must travel with the statement it answered

Horizon/lojix rewrite charter: Horizon stays the simple projection surface (a for-now hack), NOT a full triad component; lojix is the traditional component receiving the full triad-engine and schema-based-component port and carries the runtime triad. Goal: finish the lean rewrite to cutover, retire the dual Stack A/Stack B deploy burden, reach parity then switch over per node. Port high-confidence production CriomOS changes into next lojix/Horizon immediately where the correct change is clear, then test those builds.

Every agent commits and pushes at the end of every report-shaped or code-shaped pass, before signing off. The psyche reviews Git to see what landed; a dirty working copy at pass-end means the work isn't finished.

NOTA extension is type-directed structural matching over raw-parser nodes (preserving delimiters, counts, sigils, nesting). The structural macro node is a NOTA enum decoded by shape: codec matches variants in declaration order (most-specific first, first wins), recursively; encode emits the matching block (bidirectional). Realize via #[derive(StructuralMacroNode)] with per-variant shape attributes, not a runtime registry or string-name dispatch. Macros are typed NOTA records (name, structural-match pattern, capture specs, position), dispatched as an ordered list with conflict detection at registry construction. Pattern language is a typed enum of primitives (arity, atom-case, atom-sigil, delimiter-type, atom literal) so macro data stays serializable.

Hand-written Rust is ONLY the engine logic - how to process the objects, the decision-making bodies of actor methods. Everything structural emits from schema: NOTA-encoded form, rkyv binary form, all data type definitions, all enum variants, all field accessors. The engine code does not reinvent the data-structure wheel; it consumes schema-emitted types and writes only logic.

Reports are a small load-bearing set, not an accumulating archive; history lives in jj/git. Context maintenance reduces report count without losing information by agglomerating per topic: multiple reports become one Refresh report (the Capital variant tag after the number) preserving un-contradicted, un-superseded substance in better form; source reports are then deleted with the Refresh report as landing witness.

Schema is the macro-language source of truth for component data, wire, storage, and upgrade behavior. All Spirit, Signal, Nexus, and SEMA operations are declared in schema files first; generated schema types are the source of every operation data type, and handwritten Rust only implements behavior on those generated nouns.

Component Sema databases (daemon durable state) get native version-controlled server-backed atomic durability with no state loss, built once as a reusable library of generic types and traits every component can opt into. Dolt-informed; strict-typed hard-migration-per-schema-change is the core constraint, exact generic mechanism settled by prior-art research.

Domain names must be self-explanatory and carry their meaning in the variant name itself. There is no separate gloss or description layer annotating what a domain means; if a name is unclear, rename it rather than annotate it

The reusable version-control design's IntakePolicy is a universal reusable admission interface, with per-component implementations. It is not only a rebase hook; every path that introduces entries to a branch should route through it unless a later design explicitly supersedes this.

A domain scope must be a typed nested prefix of the Domain enum written in the same nested paren form as a domain value, for example Technology containing Software containing Quality, not a flat vector of free string segments. The deployed DomainScope as a vector of strings is untyped and inconsistent with typed domain values; each segment should be a real enum variant so an invalid or misspelled segment is rejected at parse time

All Rust production functions are methods on non-zero-sized data-bearing structs/enums, or trait methods (preferred). Forbidden: free functions, impls on zero-sized placeholder/marker structs used as namespaces (a free function in disguise), and module-level const-fn and async-fn. Exempt: functions under cfg-test and fn main. The rule forces four decisions per author: what objects exist, where data lives, where logic lives, what methods belong on what. Carry it strongly in the rust skill.

The intent unit is the domain: a specific grounded subject grouped under broad areas (terms category and topic retired). Domain is a closed enum with a broad category-theory starting set, represented as grouped schema enums by area (e.g. Home carrying Housing, Maintenance, and siblings) rather than one flat enum or free-text.

There should be no NOTA between components. Daemons and components exchange binary protocol data; the CLI is the translation/debugging surface that can wrap a normal call in a debugging request with options such as where logs should be displayed or stored. *IPC*

Trace code is optional at compile time so spirit-next builds without it; Nix exposes differentiated packages for lean builds vs trace-enabled testing builds. Build configuration is itself a NOTA struct (same single-NOTA-argument rule as daemon/CLI), with testing-build a field switching production/testing modes.

NOTA + schema is pure data specification, more specific than Rust (which mixes logic with data), like CapnProto's own spec language. They are the deliberate half-step from text-as-pseudocode toward Sema, the fully-correct system where code and data are typed structure. NOTA always corresponds to a specified object; the chain runs all the way down — schema is data, specifies data types, emits Rust object shapes, and operating on those via SEMA can edit the schema.

Guardian does semantic judgment on every guarded write; deterministic code gathers context, validates structure, applies typed verdict consequences. It judges a submission across justification-read dimensions (justifies intent, correct domain, certainty/importance fit) and judges a cross-record operation whole as one yes/no. Supersede atomically replaces a retired set with multiple new intents; guardian checks the new set together preserves the kernel. Each op type gets a per-op prompt with worked accept/reject examples (over-train then scale back), and always returns well-formed NOTA via generated verdict grammar, closed rejection set, parse-and-retry, shape test. A clean-context specialized guardian beats the submitting agent even on a weaker model.

Component upgrade substrate is Nix-flake versions, universal to every component: each component's flake captures running-production (last known-working main, pinned), local in-development, and named variants (unstable, testing) as inputs; all upgrade sequences are expressed in this flake-input-as-deployed-version structure, and a release tags the whole dependency surface it uses. Tests run through Nix, so commit-first — uncommitted local state is invisible to the evaluator. The endgame is a workspace-owned content-addressed vertical stack: our own content-addressed store replaces Nix store-signing, composing Criome (auth), forge (build, replacing Nix builders), and sema-upgrade (DB migration) so every layer from build to authentication to distribution is in the persona vocabulary.
Spirit ordinary agent-facing replies should not include database markers; database markers should be returned only by an explicit operation that asks for marker state.

DomainScope uses ordinary schema-emitted recursive enum semantics, staying typed all the way through matching, expansion, and equivalence. Generated code never hand-parses path shortcuts, hides stop markers, or degrades values into Debug strings or Vec<String> paths.

Center design on schema types: components' data types are schema-defined and reasoned about through their schemas, and schema-emitted types thread end to end in logic rather than being re-derived by hand. Prefer value enums that name a field's closed set of cases over stringly-typed fields. A struct field's role is its type, so no two fields share a type (dimensional correctness: Height vs Width); field-name defaults to type-name and an explicit dot-prefixed name signals a missing type or a collection (repetition is a keyed collection, never repeated fields). Schema struct declarations are positional lists of types mirroring NOTA reading order; the name-value struct form and star shorthand are retired. TypeReference application uses the flat head form (Map K V).

Counselor and assistant pair like designer and operator: counselor advises on structure (the psyche's personal, business, family, friends logistics); assistant executes. The pair handles the psyche's private affairs by default.

Tests prove real behavior: every pipeline step is genuinely implemented end-to-end and does its real job; green tests alone are not correctness. Audit tests for genuineness, prefer real-binary-via-Nix proof over in-process synthetic state, and when blocked build the minimal real version of the missing piece IN the test. Forbid stubs-dressed-as-done, circular golden tests re-reading the engine's own output, per-file readers standing in for the shared codec, and text-round-trip black-boxes.

Tests across the nota/schema/sema stack load real separate .nota/.schema/.sema files via a small shared read-fixture helper, never inline source strings. Every .schema fixture is NOTA-legal, parsable by nota-next before lowering, and uses local relative imports. Real file-based tests prove each step: structural parse, the shared nota-next codec, schema-of-schemas read, macro assembly into macro-free Asschema data, the roots model, Rust datatype/derive emission over the shared codec, and the actor system running full constraints on spirit-next.
NOTA spells unspaced multi-word string atoms in camelCase like theSouthField, mirroring PascalCase for type variants, never kebab-case

Build event surfaces, not polling: producers push events and consumers subscribe; when a mechanism only offers polling, escalate into the stack to add a real event surface rather than tuning intervals.

Schema migration is a workspace-wide structural prerequisite for every deployed persona triad (not just spirit): a contract or storage-schema change requires the running daemon and its redb to migrate coherently. The deploy-restart-update flow means a daemon meeting an existing redb must find no drift or have a sema-upgrade path; without it, any contract edit after first deploy breaks the next restart.

The workspace is an intent-and-design engine: designer and operator are two halves of one dance — implement when intent is clear and design is good enough. Design proves one prototype capability at a time in a worktree, each iteration growing both the prototype and the components it uses. On a ratified problem both implement independently; comparison integrates — convergence settles the design, divergence surfaces psyche questions, operator adopts whatever is better. Working worktree mockups beat prose reports.

Schema is the programmable composability layer over NOTA: it interprets delimiter-based, quote-free NOTA into typed input-output interfaces for actor interactions. NOTA is a thin structure-sensing library (is_parenthesis, holds_N_objects) carrying no meaning; the schema/macro layer interprets it. Schemas express composable data types and data-driven behavior trees (node-graphs) for Signal, Nexus, SEMA, and sub-engine behavior, and are a reusable declaration bedrock, not only a Spirit contract format. Same NOTA text means different things under different schemas.

Chat replies must always print the FULL RELATIVE PATH of any report(s) produced in the session, not just the short slash-number locator. Example: instead of just /369 use reports/designer/369-comparison-designer-368-vs-operator-205-running-concepts-2026-05-26.md - makes the report findable from a chat-only quote, removes the lookup step for the psyche, and matches the path discipline already used inline (skills/intent-clarification.md etc). Per psyche 2026-05-26.

The NOTA brace {} is a strict key-value map: every entry is exactly a key plus a value, no single-token entries. Key-value-ness is low-level NOTA structure (macros may consume it at schema positions). Keys form a typed enum where case plus sigil selects the variant and dispatches the value type. Namespace body is {TypeName TypeDefinition}; struct body is {fieldName TypeRef}; derived names use a value-side marker like (Derive).

The versioned operation log is the authoritative source of truth for component Sema state, and the redb store becomes a rebuildable materialized view folded from the log. This kernel inversion is chosen for the first version-control implementation rather than deferred.

Design at the post-agent capability frontier. Agents now make previously-impractical engineering routine, including zero-downtime complex database migrations and large software-stack rewrites in short time, so designs should target the best end-shape rather than a historically-practical compromise and should reach for the strongest known ideas.

Every load-bearing intent statement manifests as a constraint test (falsifiable spec or type-system witness per skills/architectural-truth-tests.md) that fails if the intent is not honored — structural/behavioral pins, not principles-in-docs. Audits feed bead filing toward constraint-tightening and end-to-end sandboxed daemon-to-daemon integration coverage.

A component contract (signal-<component>, meta-signal-<component>) carries only wire messaging vocabulary: Signal Input/Output roots, their record types, and the wire codec. Nexus and SEMA stay daemon-internal; SignalEngine/NexusEngine/SemaEngine live in the daemon, which imports the contract Input/Output. Clients send and receive only Signal messages.

Tracing is a schema-defined interface with closed generated enum vocabularies for trace names and events, not an ad-hoc string log. Trace names are macro-emitted from the schema enum variant structure, which already owns the activated object's identifier; instrumentation records only that object name, not rich per-boundary payload snapshots.

The assembled schema is a live serializable artifact — NotaDecode, NotaEncode, and rkyv on Asschema and all substructure — with the in-memory typed Rust value as bootstrap only; the Rust emitter consumes the serialized assembled form (checked-in or build-time-emitted .asschema NOTA files), not the in-memory value directly.

Valid lane shapes: <role>, ordinal second-/third-<role> for parallel capacity (copying the whole following role), and qualified-prefix-<role> (system-, cluster-, pi-). Specialized lanes inherit their base role (system-specialist becomes system-operator). Each agent uses its exact harness role-name; -assistant and -specialist suffixes are retired.

Re-importing vetted records into a fresh Spirit database needs a privileged meta-signal Import operation that writes a record directly, preserving its identifier, timestamp, and fields, and bypasses the guardian. Guardian-bypassing writes live only on the meta-signal contract. The working signal stays gated.
A NOTA enum variant with an optional empty payload renders as a data-carrying record such as (Technology None), not as a bare Technology atom.

The entire @-binder surface is abandoned; nota-next removes all @-binding parser support. The authored schema surface is the positional bracket/brace form ({...} struct bodies, [...] enum/variant lists), which rejects @ at source. The root schema value stays an implicit filename-named struct; legacy pipe declarations are transitional, replaced by the positional form.

Match effort to the request and stay within the asked scope: when the psyche asks whether something is possible or to research tools, deliver the research and assessment then stop — implementation requires explicit instruction. Size effort to the ask: a simple review or what-do-you-think wants a lightweight read-and-judge pass or single agent, reserving expensive adversarial fan-out for explicit thoroughness requests or work too large for one context. When work surfaces many open psyche questions, lean on pattern-recognition of captured intent and propose an MVP matching the letter of intent, surfacing only the truly-unanswerable remainder rather than dozens of inferable questions.

Drop the persona- prefix from component crate names workspace-wide: spirit/router/message/mind/orchestrate/harness/terminal/introspect/system/engine-management rename persona-X → X (and signal-persona-X → signal-X, owner-signal-persona-X → owner-signal-X). Exceptions keeping persona-: agent-harness components (persona-codex, persona-claude, persona-pi, persona-gemini, persona-open-code) since they are agent-process harnesses, not architectural-pattern components. Persona itself stays persona — that is its name, not a prefix.

Three schema types for three runtime planes — Signal (wire), Nexus (execution IO/UI), SEMA (durable state). Each plane has its own engine and traits but shares one pattern: run code over an input message, return an output message. Rust emission splits the planes into separate namespaces so payloads use plain names (Input, Output) without plane prefixes.

Spirit entries carry exactly two orthogonal axes: Certainty (confidence the statement is true) and Importance (how much it matters). They are never conflated and neither substitutes for the other — a tentative idea can be very-low certainty yet high importance. Importance (formerly Weight) is declared directly by the psyche, not only accumulated through reinforcement; a statement made once can carry high importance. No separate Weight field.

Technology divides into two parallel clusters, Hardware and Software — the true split of technology. Every child of Technology is a cluster with no bare leaves, so all hardware is a queryable scope mirroring the Software cluster.

Schema files live with their contract's source-of-truth; a repo may hold multiple .schema files (one per contract), named <crate>/<contract-name>.schema. Wire/signal schemas live in signal-<component> as the cross-component compilation boundary Rust sees as one canonical source; stable public signal schemas sit in contract repos separate from daemon logic so consumers aren't rebuilt on internal changes. Storage-contract and internal actor-message schemas live with the daemon (daemon+database+internal actors are one process/compilation unit). A daemon's configuration type lives in the signal contract (imported for binary startup decode; meta-signal Configure wraps it), not hand-written in the daemon. Signal repos carry only signal/wire vocabulary; runtime-plane, storage, provider-effect, and REST schema belong in the runtime component.

Foundational schema model (supersedes the at-sigil for macro invocation): EVERYTHING is a struct — unit variant = zero-field struct, data variant = one-field struct, enum = single-field struct holding the active variant. A schema node is a tagged variant (paren: first object = tag picking the variant of the known expected type, second = carried data), read against the schema-of-schemas, so positional typing resolves variant-vs-macro with no sigil. A macro is a Rust struct; macro-space is a tagged union of them; invoking is a tagged variant against the known macro type. The schema is one recursive shape: a root struct with macro-expanded fields down to scalar leaves; macros expand structurally by position; a delimiter shape (e.g. bracket field vector) means struct/newtype only at the lowerer's position; recursion terminates at built-in macros over scalar leaves (ints, unit-variant enums, bools, typed-newtype strings).
Psyche-facing agents must keep intent-logging guidance fresh in context, and using Spirit as the intent tool requires loading intent guidance first.

One consistent cryptographic basis spans the entire version-control and backup system: blake3 for all content addressing and Criome BLS (BLS12-381 curve, BLS signature scheme) for signing and attesting history; no component diverges. The pre-Criome agent-identity path uses BLS12-381 keypairs (not Ed25519) from day one so identity transitions cleanly when Criome lands.

Intent capture is modeled as a court of law: the submitting agent advocates, psyche verbatim quotes (asterisk-bracketed spans) are testimony, the guardian is judge rendering a binary verdict. The justification is a strongly-typed argued case (typed testimony with optional antecedents plus reasoning for why certainty/domain/kind/importance fit), never a stringly blob; the claim is the operation's own Entry. Certainty is the burden of proof the quotes must clear on modal strength: over-claimed certainty is rejected back to the highest-thinking model to reword, the guardian never silently lowers it, preserving psyche modality. Ship the lean core first (typed testimony plus support check, binary verdict), deferring the elaborate court structure until it demonstrably falls short.

The endpoint of the meta-work is the final software engine: an engine that self-improves to the point where nothing better could ever be made. The eventually-impossible-to-improve software of the workspace essence is reached through the engine improving itself, not only through human-directed refinement.

A contract is a channel; each channel gets its own schema (wire/repo contract, storage contract, each internal-actor-message vocabulary), so one component has multiple .schema files, one per channel — superseding one-schema-per-component. A component is at least three plane schemas: the Signal wire contract lives wire-only in the contract repo; Nexus and SEMA plane schemas are separate .schema files inside the daemon crate (e.g. cloud/schema/nexus.schema), each with own imports/exports/namespace and importing the wire Signal IO. Plane schemas are not separate crates/repos. Schemas warrant from need-to-interact: every part worth describing gets a schema the psyche can inspect and interact with, and signal-frame itself is schema.
The policy and control socket in triad components should be called the meta socket, not the owner socket. *IPC*

A method call on an actor IS the interaction: InteractTrait and the interaction-actor concept are retracted, schema-rust emits no Interact/InteractionActor traits. Mediator survives only as ordinary actor-method topology (M's method body decides). Surviving: two-languages distinction (internal effect vs external wire), effect-table match-driven dispatch, actor fan-out as method returns.

Schema-to-Rust lowering is token-based via Rust's real macro infra (quote!/proc-macro2 TokenStream/ToTokens), not a hand-rolled string emitter or god-struct writer. Each schema noun (RustStruct, RustEnum, RustField, TypeExpression) renders itself recursively; cross-object concerns (plane split/projections) live in a PlaneType the nouns consult. Checked-in .rs vs inline expansion is a separate visibility choice.

Signal is NOTA-free: signal-frame and the signal-component / meta-signal-component contract crates carry binary/rkyv only, with no NOTA encoding or projection on request/reply types. NOTA is a separate text-edge concern living outside signal.

A Spirit short identifier is the shortest-unique base36-lowercase prefix of the record's random hash, minimum three characters, scoped per kind, extended only on same-kind collision. The full random hash stays binary on the wire; only the short prefix appears in text.

Schema is purely positional: a name appears only where something is genuinely keyed (a namespace map entry by its key, a sum variant by its tag); a positional slot — a struct's fields, the Input/Output enum positions — is bare because position already says what it is. Struct bodies are positional lists of field types and reject the old field-Type pair syntax; an explicitly different field name uses the dot differentiator (source.TypeReference), but dot syntax is invalid when the explicit role equals the type-derived name (Entry topic.Topic rejected, use Entry Topic).

Agglomerating repeated or related intent preserves source provenance and may preserve accumulated importance through weight where that axis exists, but does not automatically raise certainty; certainty rises only when the synthesized statement is itself better supported or stated with higher confidence.

Create the cloud component as the cloud API management triad, with Cloudflare DNS, Cloudflare settings, and redirect rules as the first targets (redirect after DNS if the surface supports it cleanly). The surface splits Mutate verbs onto meta-signal-cloud (privileged, owner authority) and Query verbs onto signal-cloud (public): Cloudflare is a state the cloud daemon reflects, refresh-by-querying is public, mutations require owner authority — generalising to other providers and any component whose state is a reflected external resource.

The criome/lojix cluster is validated by a fully-networked multi-node test substrate with the spirit gate authenticated end-to-end through one reusable interface. One identical propagation test targets three substrates: a default hermetic host-untouched runNixOSTest VM cluster on prometheus; an opt-in durable on-demand microvm node on prometheus (BootOnce-activated, a typed horizon-rs NodeService, router/production untouched); and opt-in DigitalOcean droplets for cross-machine validation. The lojix production-cutover e2e deploys a full OS into a throwaway KVM VM (prometheus MAY carry the typed VmHost capability) and proves it survives SSH disconnect. The gate accepts an authorized head and rejects an unauthorized one fail-closed, building from 1-of-1 local toward multi-machine quorum.

Spirit record identity is a stable, non-reusable, opaque random handle assigned once at creation, frozen and never reused (not a content-address fingerprint, since records are mutable, and not reusable incremental numbers). Concretely: 96-bit CSPRNG values rendered as lowercase base36 shortest-unique-prefix codes, minimum three characters per record kind. Recency tracking uses daemon-stamped time, not the id.

Cloud's Plan operation is renamed Mutate, its acknowledged-state reply Mutated. The daemon distinguishes Mutate-sent (request issued to provider, pending) from Mutated (provider-acknowledged); held state is last-known-acknowledgment, not a live provider query.

Report lanes are per-role and divide write authority and research context: an agent writes only in its own reports role directory — except when the psyche explicitly instructs it for one specific task (not normalized after). Reports are exempt from claim flow; claim only for shared coordination surfaces or files outside the exemption.

Provenance and agglomeration are expressed through a relations field on records — a vector of short non-colliding record-id hashes pointing to source/related records (a Correction relates to what it corrects) — not a dedicated Composite record type. This lets repeated or related intent be agglomerated into a newer, stronger record without losing provenance; refreshing several intents may yield one merged record or two-or-three records of different kinds.

Report filename convention is reports/<role>/<N>-<Variant>-<topic>-<date>.md: a capitalized Variant naming the report kind (Psyche/Design/Audit/Research/Synthesis/Closeout/Handover) plus one or more hyphen-separated topic labels between number and title, reports staying in their lane subdirectory. Topics come from a per-workspace controlled vocabulary and enable filename topic-grep without opening files. Documented in skills/reporting.md.

Define assembled schema (.asschema) before sugar: it is macro-free final data — Vec, Optional, Enum, Struct, Newtype, Plain, Carries are final assembled-schema variants, not macro invocations. Sugar belongs only in authored schema before lowering. Schema macros re-emit schema structure toward AssembledSchema (conceptually re-emitting NOTA, intermediate may stay binary) and must expand into a fully one-to-one assembled tree describing all generated types, endpoint variant names, and carried input structs before Rust is emitted. Application is iterative to a fixed point: each pass applies lowerers at macro positions, macros may introduce new macros, until the namespace is pure typed enums, structs, newtypes.

The schema language handles only the workspace Rust subset: named-field structs, named-variant enums (bare, data-carrying, struct-variant, nested), newtype (single-unnamed-field) structs, and scalar primitives (unit enums, booleans, integers, strings, vectors). No tuples — tuples are poorly-defined structs and excluded; no multi-field tuple structs, anonymous tuples, or field-less anonymous types. Strings are conceptually vectors of characters behind boxed storage; domain concepts should be typed beyond raw String (typed-string newtypes), and EnumIdentifier is a core constrained-string type.

Home/desktop activation safety: a Home-profile activation persists across reboot (a reboot restoring an older deployed Claude version is a bug to investigate), keeps the active graphical session and user logged in, and preserves active agent sessions — live Home/Niri/desktop deploys with ongoing agents proceed only when the user confirms the disruption window. Pi operator-safety proceeds without asking merely because a repository is dirty.

Component feedback, status, and errors are typed self-descriptive NOTA enums and structs whose names carry the meaning — a result type named well enough to need no message string. Our feedback language is enums/structs decoded through NOTA, specializing strings-only-at-the-edges to the messaging surface.

Define the ASSEMBLED schema first: the canonical, macro-free, sugar-free FINAL data model Rust is generated from — processable as ordinary data, not a hidden parser control path. TypeReference is a sum (Plain, Vector, Map, Optional); TypeDeclaration is a sum (Struct, Enum, Newtype) — all final forms. It is a typed Rust data model with a NOTA projection, carrying fully-qualified module-unique names so it can reference types across modules/crates. It strictly and faithfully represents data as stored: load-bearing field order stays visible; use the real product/sum/map/scalar shape, never homogeneous containers for heterogeneous fields or empty wrapper records. Lock the assembled model first; design surface sugar only after the target exists.

Lojix is the production deploy path: execute the cutover now — the lojix daemon becomes production and legacy lojix-cli retires per node, standing down the parallel Stack A / Stack B burden (piloted at parity on ouranos). A lojix deploy/build is durable: a job actor owns the external process and persists state, surviving client disconnect; cancellation is declared per operation (durable deploys survive disconnect by default, speculative queries/builds may opt into kill-on-drop). Keep using lojix while making the workload safe; the risk is local build/eval resource pressure. Sandbox passing precedes the lean-stack cutover to main.

Spirit should not call the referent guardian when a referent registration request names an already registered referent; existing registry state settles that request without LLM judgment. *admission control*

A newtype in the assembled schema is a single-element brace with just the wrapped type — (Public Topic { String }), not a derived field name. TypeValue carries a distinct Newtype(TypeReference) variant: brace with one element lowers to Newtype; alternating name-type pairs lower to Struct. Rust emits a tuple-struct pub struct Topic(pub String) with NotaTransparent (Topic([foo]) and topic.0). Surface @-syntax: Topic@String or Topic@{ String }.

Every component derives two vocabularies from one .schema file: an internal effect language for engine-internal actors over each other and storage (actor mediator surface), and an external wire language for signal contracts via signal sockets (signal-frame envelope, ShortHeader, dispatch). The signal_channel macro emits all three: wire surface, sema operations, and sema lowering operations.
NOTA encode/decode derives are optional generated surfaces, applied only where needed; daemon-only components stay binary-only (rkyv signal frames) and carry no NOTA decoding code.

Universal Magnitude type in the shared typed-record crate (signal-core/signal-sema) replaces all small ordinal enums (e.g. Certainty). Eight variants: Zero, Minimum, VeryLow, Low, Medium, High, VeryHigh, Maximum; Zero (the neutral absent rung, chosen over Option/None) declared first so derived Ord places it lowest. Fixed-byte rkyv discriminant makes width free; consumption subset is per-component policy. Spirit reads certainty=Zero as removal-candidate; certainty stays optional at the persona-spirit entry boundary with absence semantics local to Spirit rather than a None inside shared vocabulary.

In schema-next, a bare @-sigil type at a field/variant position derives the binding name from the type: @Topics ≡ topics@Topics (field=camelCase of type), @(Vec Topic) from the composite, @SomethingHoldingSomething a data variant named after the held type. Bare PascalCase at an enum position = unit variant; explicit name@Type still works. EnumVariants macro supports compact self-tagged variants (one-element paren = tag-and-type; paren name with brace body = named struct, bracket body = named enum); inline enum-body names resolve unit vs data against the type table (a type ⇒ data-carrying). Assembled model unchanged: Vec<(Name, TypeReference)> for fields, named Variant for enum variants; derivation at lowering.

The agent component models LLM/cloud providers as a generic OpenAI-compatible API (endpoint, model, key as config), so adding a provider (e.g. DeepSeek) is configuration not a contract change; deployment surfaces supply config and secrets. Support may be build-time opt-in; capability observation distinguishes built-but-unconfigured from not-built, and unsupported requests return a typed unsupported-capability reply.

A dedicated program-agnostic session-control component, named terminal-control (contracts signal-terminal-control and meta-signal-terminal-control), owns the terminal session surface: durable name-to-socket session registry, signal control-plane relay, per-session prompt-pattern registry, and session-observation Sema. It is folded into neither harness (AI-only, would orphan non-harness cells) nor orchestrate (which owns coordination not delivery identity); orchestrate owns cell-instance lifecycle policy as an order plane. terminal-cell stays the abduco primitive and absorbs the input-gate writer and prompt-matcher currently duplicated with terminal.
The root type of a schema is the message surface (what is sent); each schema declares its own input and output enums and uses colon-path namespace imports for shared types.

Schema is self-hosting all-the-way-down: NOTA's own grammar and types are described by the foundational schema, which generates the Rust that interprets NOTA; the authoring language is itself a macro expanding to assembled-schema data, so the schema-of-schemas generates its own assembled and short-syntax forms. Schema is the load-bearing crystallization of earlier ASCII/component work — self-describing code, wire format, and docs from one .schema file.

QEMU/nixosTest VM checks run only on a node carrying the typed VM-testing service and VmHost KVM-hosting capability; the runner is selected by that service capability, never by whichever machine an agent runs on. Agents verify the VM-testing host boundary before invoking qemu-backed checks or ask for the correct host; ordinary nodes remain compile/evaluation surfaces. Generalizes the heavy-work-placement rule to VM testing.

Separate a thing's categorical KIND (one-of-N essence with no off) from its additive CAPABILITY vector (the things it does). Kind is an explicit categorical choice with no implicit defaults — mutually-exclusive, validated at projection with a typed error — never modelled as pre-selected defaults an additive vector overrides, since an additive vector cannot express un-selection. Additive vectors for what you turn on; categorical labels for the one-of-N essence.

Secret paths are scoped to the resource they serve: local-service tokens are scoped to the zone where the service lives (distinct from provider-scoped paths like cloudflare/api-token that are global to the provider). The local LLM API token's stable gopass path is goldragon.criome/local-llm-api-token — zone-scoped not host-scoped — so it survives the largeAI role moving between hosts.

LLM prompt and template prose lives in separate plain-text files, not embedded in Rust code; the code includes them at build time via include_str so the binary stays self-contained while the prose is edited as data. Document the file layout in the architecture file.

One typed noun per semantic object across the schema/NOTA stack: source text and checked-in/serialized artifact share the same datatype unless there's a real semantic projection. Assembled schema is one typed Asschema object projected to rkyv bytes (SEMA db), binary archive, and .asschema NOTA text via the same Nota-derived types; a SchemaMacro source entry fits one serializable enum variant, not split source/data enums. Schema source itself is a typed value with a dedicated in/out codec reading strict NOTA source into source-node data and writing it back canonically, distinct from raw NOTA parsing and from assembled serialization.

A schema declares its roots at one designated point only — the named entry-point set generalizing fixed input/output into a roots collection; nesting adds no further roots. Everything else is a free datatype declared in the namespace and referenced by roots at some position. This shapes the assembled schema as a roots section plus a free-datatype-declarations section, matching the operator roots model.

The wire protocol is the Signal Protocol; its messages move through a universal mail mechanism (mailer/dispatcher/push system) shared by every component. Asynchronous representation lives at the data-type level: message data types themselves carry correlation IDs and lifecycle state tracking sent/queued/processing/replied transitions.

Spirit gets an explicit capability to remove intent entries beyond supersession/negation, plus first-class removal-candidate review: a collect-removal-candidates observation flow that uses record certainty/magnitude to surface low-confidence records and archives or emits reviewed Zero-certainty records before retracting them from the hot store.
Version-pair vocabulary: current/main (deployed production version) versus proposal/dev/next (new version being introduced)

Deploying to the large-AI node (CriomOS role large-ai-node, NodeService::LargeAi — today prometheus, running hostapd and dnsmasq) must preserve its network connectivity: a live switch restarts those services and drops the connection (broke the gemma deploy). Use the safe BootOnce / boot-mode activation path rather than Switch until console/out-of-band access and sign-off exist, and avoid staging an unverified generation for an unintended reboot. Name the role not the host — refresh existing Prometheus references that should be role-level. FullOS deploys update the production CriomOS flake lock before deploying Zeus.

A loaded schema file's root struct shape is known a priori from the root KIND (base vs reactive) signaled outside the bytes (extension/load context), like a Nix derivation. The file supplies only positional VALUES at known slots — no root, field, or type names. A base file is just its namespace; a reactive file is bare input enum, output enum, namespace, no Input/Output labels even in root.schema.

Visuals in reports are mermaid diagrams (no ASCII text-block diagrams) and belong in reports not chat, where they may be used freely. Keep diagrams short and focused — 4-8 nodes, hard cap ~10; split bigger substance into multiple single-question diagrams (data flow, type lineage, control flow, failure modes) so each reads at a glance. Node labels say what the node IS in 2-5 words; keep bead IDs and file paths out of nodes, in prose or a sibling table. nota/schema design graphs pair each graph with its code surface and ground each scenario in Nix tests.

NOTA mirrors the rkyv self-describing root + relative-pointer box layout: the root stays compact (sized fields inline) while unsized/growing fields (String, Vec, Option-of-unsized, nested records) become BOXES appended after the root in declaration order, so no box-index naming is needed. Coordinate (vector-N element-M) reads the M-th element of the N-th box vector, a direct projection of the binary form.

Roll the whole workspace forward to latest intent. Migration completes only when the superseded implementation, dependency, and execution path are removed (wrapping an old path is not migration). Drop compatibility surfaces — the Asschema projection, old Asschema APIs, old CLI/wire names — once superseded; new-Spirit production-readiness owes no interface compat. Replace low-authority draft evidence; archive dead/duplicate/stub repos; orchestrate becomes the triad-built production replacement. One test substrate at a time, docs and beads track the universal direction.

Components have two schema categories. EXTERNAL schemas describe surfaces outside daemon runtime: wire contracts to other components (signal-vocabulary surviving the socket boundary) and database/storage contracts (state surviving process exit). INTERNAL schemas describe surfaces within runtime: actor message vocabularies and inter-actor channel contracts sharing process memory. Multiple schemas per component, each describing ONE channel; major actors get a schema. Internal-schema changes break neither database nor wire.

prometheus enforces API auth via a gopass-fed token; both prometheus's api-key file and client wrappers read from the same gopass entry, and a mint tool generates the token and writes it to that gopass path.
A CLI-with-NOTA structurally forces the daemon protocol to be binary: the CLI translates NOTA into the binary protocol calls the daemon receives.

terminal-cell is an abduco-style per-application attached wrapper: one running application in one PTY, owning the append-only transcript, persisting only to allow viewer detach/reattach. Process-per-application is the confirmed model. It is not a triad engine component nor a managed service daemon — its non-triad shape is incidental (predates the triad engine). OPEN: which component owns the terminal control/session surface (naming, registry, viewer-adapter, input-gate, signal-terminal control plane, durable session metadata) is still under research.

Zero certainty is the removal-candidate state: a Zero record has no value and must not surface by default. The default Observe query excludes Zero; queries take a certainty floor defaulting to Minimum (excludes Zero), and seeing Zero records requires an explicit floor (Zero/Any). This unifies removal-candidate with zero certainty.

In the assembled schema a struct is a key-value brace map: field name is the key, type reference is the value — the long-form that struct sugar expands into. Field names and type names are unified: a field's identity IS its type name, derived once and lowered to lowercase (camelCase) in code while the type stays PascalCase (struct Entry's Kind reference -> field kind: Kind). Type declarations are visibility-tagged Public(name,value) or Private(name,value): Public is exported for library use; Private, local, and inline are not. An inline PascalCase type in a field is sugar — derive the field name from the type, log the inline type as a Private local declaration (module-local non-exported Rust type).

Every component carries a meta slot so configuration is unreachable via the ordinary working-signal surface; if no separate meta-signal repo exists the component repo itself carries the meta-signal surface. Split a contract into separate signal-component and meta-signal-component repos for: rebuild-churn isolation (peers depend on the small contract repo, recompile only on wire-contract changes), security visibility (owner-only ops in a distinct meta repo non-owner clients need not depend on), and optionality (componentless owner relationship needs only the ordinary signal contract). The split is compilation/dependency isolation and authority clarity, not where state or logic lives.

Schema macro expansion ends in AssembledSchema (not SchemaMid): a pure NOTA-representable assembled-file type with fully resolved enum/struct definitions, typed endings, no remaining macros. It is the complete input to Rust generation — including NOTA text and portable rkyv binary for SEMA and signal formats — and may later be driven from a single macro reading repo schema files.

Tracing belongs in the schema-generated engine traits themselves via default derived/no-op trait implementations, so the trace hooks live on the interface/actor contract and trace builds override or install a sink — not as separate SignalTrace, NexusTrace, SemaTrace side traits.

Message lifecycle exposes hookable events: the mail dispatch system emits/commits a typed MessageSent action at the message-sent boundary, firing as soon as a message is sent so hooks, UI, observers, routers, and subscribers can react immediately.

Psyche reports speak to a human in a narrative voice: every chat response paraphrases an accompanying per-response report, carrying 3-7 top items spread across (a) intent questions/clarifications, (b) how-new-mechanisms-work observations, (c) recent-work examples. Use Spirit IDs to mark ranges of intent, not as per-claim inline citations.

Preferred topology: one designer + one operator double-lane per workspace, communicating through the psyche who routes between them. The operator-assistant lane is second-operator (parallel-main authority, not support), mirroring designer/second-designer; both base disciplines carry two parallel-main lanes by default.

Components with two authority surfaces use the two-contract triad: two schemas (signal-<c> working/peer-callable, meta-signal-<c> policy/owner-only), each emitting its own Signal/Nexus/SEMA engines and sharing record types; the runtime imports both and runs two listeners on two sockets. lojix ships two CLIs: a meta-socket CLI for meta-signal-lojix (Deploy/Pin/Unpin/Retire, daemon config, owner SO_PEERCRED-auth) and a regular-socket CLI for signal-lojix peer ops (Query/WatchDeployments/WatchCacheRetention/Unwatch). cloud uses signal-cloud (read-only Observe/Validate) and meta-signal-cloud (eight account/plan mutations). cloud and domain components use the current triad signal/sema/executor architecture; future component work uses this latest architecture.

schema-codegen capability set is closed (types, generic frame expansion, payload structs/newtypes/enums, standard impls, role/marker traits, shape-computed constants, method-bearing trait surface, actor wiring, plane carriers, opt-in Deref). Next phase is integration/migration, not language design: flip scalar-newtype impls default-on, integrate onto code-repo main, and port all components — regenerate spirit, signal-spirit, meta-signal-spirit and the triad through the emitter, swapping generated Rust for hand-wired boilerplate. Pipeline: each plane defined in schema, assembled into Asschema, emitted as Rust datatypes plus basic trait impls; only abstract engine bodies (decide, validate) stay hand-written as the decision-making actor system over schema-emitted nouns. Spirit is a copyable contract-daemon/engine-stack exemplar, not an all-in-one pilot. Runtime binaries stay small carrying only strict rkyv wire/storage contracts; the schema/NOTA compiler (nota-next, schema-next, schema-rust-next) is build-time-only, never linked into runtime, and the NOTA text codec is an optional edge feature absent from the daemon.

Node NixOS configs build and realize on the target node itself (or its configured remote builder); coordinating machines evaluate only — realizing a model-bearing closure elsewhere drags tens-of-GB models onto the wrong store (extends heavy-work-placement rule 0a9p to all node builds). Large-AI model prefetches and fixed-output hash discovery run on the node (e.g. prometheus) over its wired LAN as durable target-side systemd work with a target-side periodic monitor, surviving SSH drops. Quantized variants install through the CriomOS large-AI model inventory deploy path; disruptive promotions launch as durable systemd transient units. Deploy uses lojix-cli on the host.

Assembled-schema scalar built-ins are named in full English: Integer, Boolean, String — never Bool, U64, or abbreviations. Any further scalar built-ins follow the same full-English rule. Text is reserved for a possible future string-with-spaces variant; String is the scalar in use now.

terminal-cell lifecycle ownership splits three ways: orchestrate owns cell instance lifecycle policy and ordering, systemd owns OS process supervision and restart-survival (cells fork via systemd to survive a terminal-control restart), and terminal-control owns the durable sema instance record — on restart it rediscovers running cells from sema and reattaches their control and data sockets, realizing abduco survive-detach via systemd + sema.

Proof of build/deploy/test claims must compile, execute, or round-trip the real path; grep is allowed only as a narrow negative guard proving a retired symbol or forbidden pattern is absent, never as positive proof. Test-claims cite a concrete file path, the invocation, and the witness output; the workspace audits whether claims match reality. A quiet ~27-minute local check is suspect unless it is a known heavyweight build (e.g. Rust toolchain).

Methodology for major architectural breaks: create a new repository with a -next or -v2 (or other longer descriptive) suffix rather than working on a branch of an existing repo. The longer name signals prototype/breakthrough status; the new repo can be developed in parallel without disrupting production work on the old repo; it may start as a fork. After the break stabilizes, rename to the canonical short name (replacing the original). Makes parallel development sane for agents - they work on different repos rather than navigating branch states. Per psyche 2026-05-26 directive: this methodology should be made into a basic skill.
A normal chat response carries 3-7 big items, balanced between new-design explanations and questions for the psyche.

Canonical name for the 64-bit per-message prefix is short header; every message carries it, everything else is body. Structure is 8 enums (1 root + 7 sub) filling 64 bits, discriminator info only — payload lives in the body. Version is not in the header: the engine enforces it at the database level (one schema version per database; migration changes the database, not per-message tags).
Spirit write acknowledgements are token-cheap: creation returns only the created record UID (not a SemaReceipt with UID plus database marker), and acks never echo submitted intent content.

A dispatched subagent inherits the dispatcher's lane, lock, and report-numbering slot — it writes into the dispatcher's role report subdir, never its own lane. Parallel subagents are assigned report numbers before launch; sharing the dispatcher's single lock, two cannot work the same thing.

Name the persistent typed-record store the SEMA database, not RAD or other nicknames. Sema is the workspace's pure binary domain-tree format; redb-persisted records are conceptually a sema database, holding strings (quotes, names, summaries) today that reduce as natural language becomes typed leaves. Internal database logic uses the same schema-defined message language as component signals, so a growing database component can split into its own daemon without changing the language pattern.

Spirit record privacy is an Optional field available to any record kind: a public record's privacy is None, an elevated one is (Some Magnitude). Optionality is the explicit None token, never an omitted positional field — every NOTA positional record carries every field.

The schema-derived engine mechanism is workspace-canonical: NexusWork/NexusAction asymmetric pair + five-variant action set (ReplyToSignal, CommandSemaWrite, CommandSemaRead, CommandEffect, Continue); macro-generated runner loop (triad_main! from schema-rust-next); per-component effects declared in schema (Stash first); Continue as in-process recursion; cross-component invocation via Signal contracts, not Nexus-internal access. These reaction-frame types (Work, Action, five-variant set) are universal — declared once, bound per component, never re-authored. Emitted triad engines ARE kameo actors (minimal on_start/on_stop now; backpressure/scheduling deferred); runtime nouns named Actor must be real Kameo actors. A universal Interact trait — interact(other), async when awaiting outbound queries like criome authorization — formalizes that all logic is interactions between domains.

Distinguish a lean-pending-information from a ratified Decision: a psyche statement that leans toward a choice while explicitly asking for more context is a lean (low certainty), not a ratified Decision; conservative capture understates when more-information is flagged. When the psyche marks a new statement low-certainty, search existing records on that topic and split or correct any higher-certainty record so high- and low-certainty parts are represented separately. Spirit clients do not provide capture time.

Schema source lowering rejects duplicate declarations for the same type name: when an inline declaration and another inline or trailing namespace declaration both introduce a name, it is an error, not an order-dependent override or merge.

Sema classification vocabulary stays internal to sema-engine execution and observation, off the public contract wire. Contract operation roots are domain verbs and the Sema class is daemon-derived internally; the six Sema words (Assert, Mutate, Retract, Match, Subscribe, Validate) appear neither as request-root tags nor as an AuthorizedSignalVerb enum nor as a payloadless SemaObservation event in any signal/meta-signal contract. Requires a cleanup pass across the legacy contracts carrying the pattern (signal-mind, signal-router, signal-criome, owner-signal-persona, signal-persona-spirit, signal-orchestrate).

Trace events render as NOTA at the client edge: trace event types derive a NOTA codec from their schema definitions, so display reduces to the standard NOTA encoder, not ad-hoc Display formatting — the rendered string IS NOTA text, the typed-data text projection.

Macro application and enum declaration both use the grouped form: a head plus one parenthesized payload, e.g. (Foo (A B)). In an enum declaration Foo is the declared type and A, B are variants; in a macro invocation Foo is the operator applied to grouped arguments A, B.

[] is and always has been the vector delimiter in NOTA. Schema and assembled-schema layers may read a vector at a typed position as a product/field list, but [] itself stays a vector and is never redefined as struct syntax.
NOTA string delimiters are required only for strings with spaces or forbidden symbols; bare-eligible strings (topic names, symbol-safe schema and type names) stay bare atoms.

Running a Spirit Observe is standard routine practice, not optional: agents observe recent Spirit records as a matter of course — proactively at the start of and during substantive work — to let recorded intent guide the work, not only when the Spirit gate flags intent as unclear; just do it as a guide rather than presenting it to the psyche as an open decision.

The sema version-control remote is a dedicated mirror component triad (mirror, signal-mirror, meta-signal-mirror).
