One payload-blind append-ingest mirror daemon on the ouranos tailnet host serves every component store: validates sequence continuity and expected head, deduplicates idempotently, fsyncs before acking, carries retention/privacy behind its meta signal; its own state is a sema-engine store.
Claude Code, Codex, and Pi must be updated together through CriomOS-home and the full CriomOS lock so active profiles and full-system deployments stay in sync after crashes or reboots.

The cloud component's next active capability is on-demand compute-node provisioning via cloud-hoster provider APIs, beyond the shipped Cloudflare read-only DNS path, targeting easiest-to-spin-up providers first. DigitalOcean is the lead compute provider (superseding Hetzner-lead): primary provisioning target, billing by the second/minute so create-and-destroy cycles cost actual elapsed usage and the billing-hour reuse pool is unnecessary for it. Hetzner remains supported but not lead; its hourly billing still uses a billing-hour-aware reuse pool — an idle recently-created node within its paid window is reused instead of delete-and-recreate, reaped before crossing into a new paid hour (keep-warm 59 min for Hetzner), the larger benefit being latency from skipping create/boot/install.

Intent single source of truth becomes Spirit: capture and query intent in one place, not in per-repo files. Each repo ARCHITECTURE.md is the file kept current for architectural state. Per-repo INTENT.md files migrate off into Spirit gradually, opportunistically whenever a repo is touched. Read intent now means read Spirit.

When the cloud component is built with the cloudflare feature, flarectl is a runtime dependency: cloud binaries are wrapped via makeWrapper/wrapProgram so flarectl is guaranteed on the daemon PATH at runtime, not relying on the user profile.
Run all tests through Nix CI, including schema boilerplate generation and Spirit database update validation.

The lojix deploy daemon serves connections concurrently (bounded by a permit cap), never blocking on in-progress nix builds; deploy state is per-request (each connection owns its pipeline cursor), the durable Store is the shared point locked briefly per sema op, and long nix effects hold no global lock.

Schema imports are selective by name — import specific named types from another schema namespace rather than the whole namespace, using the same names as the source (no rename needed, per-component scope prevents conflicts) and keeping concerns separated per engine/component. Import/name clashes fail within a schema context.
The active agent role is the topic of the psyche window

Spirit pilots Criome-backed operation authorization: a Spirit operation needing auth asks Criome to authorize an exact content-addressed request/log digest. criome authenticates the SUBMITTER (SO_PEERCRED caller resolved to a registered criome Identity), as an after-the-fact, non-blocking, out-of-band attestation binding caller to the per-operation digest; the spirit guardian keeps the full content verdict.

A type's kind is announced at its declaration by the delimiter wrapping its value: brace body = struct, square-bracket body = enum, never inferred from syntactic-slot position. Generics use the reserved pipe-parenthesis delimiter, Name (| [params] body |) — params the binder list, body a nested struct/enum. nota-next already parses PipeParenthesis/PipeBrace, so this is schema-level work; use sites resolve by name like Vector/Map/Optional.

NOTA encoders avoid over-bracketing: bare-safe atoms encode bare inside typed positions like vectors, bracket forms reserved for whitespace/delimiter safety; typed projection drops redundant wrapper delimiters when the enclosing head already fixes the payload shape, rendering operation-root fields directly.

When work is already authorized, execute it the right way and all the way autonomously without gating on per-step confirmation or repeated questions, and dispatch subagents to carry it; reserve asking for genuinely unresolvable forks, not already-decided or default-resolvable steps.

Development tooling for schema-derived crates should regenerate emitted Rust automatically or through one central command whenever schema files change, then rerun the relevant build or tests so agents edit schema first and consume regenerated types.
SEMA is the compact data format defined by schema.

Tooling and extensions are packaged declaratively through Nix, hiding Nix paths from internal views: Pi extension sources go in flake inputs with hashes in flake.lock and persona-Pi packages standard Pi extensions through Nix; CriomOS-home packages browser-use with a wrapper using the local Gemma model served by the large-AI node when present; video-editing agents get most normal video-editing tools through the regular medium profile (exceptions for unusually large dependencies); and a Nix utility fetches Hugging Face models by URL/query mirroring nix-prefetch-url (prefetch via the huggingface CLI, hash, write a fetcher derivation parameterised by the HF URL/query) as a workspace tool for getting any HF model into the store.

Router owns a standardized routing protocol: a router-typed envelope carries routing/object metadata for a serialized contract-owned rkyv payload, stays payload-blind beyond envelope/routing/authentication/delivery policy, and is the sole operational subscription matcher for non-direct passing. Object-update fan-out: criome stamps/authorizes the object and emits an authorized-object reference (not payload); Router matches subscriptions and fans references to subscribers who fetch the rkyv object. Accepted objects are quorum-backed by default. Time-driven pulses use an after-time contract scheduling a later check that triggers a new quorum-signed acceptance if its events did not occur. First production milestone is spirit->criome->router->mirror: Spirit asks local criome to authenticate a content-addressed object, criome verifies/signs under a cluster-root identity and propagates via Router, the remote mirror fetches/restores state — criome auth-only, Router transport-only, mirror the version-control substrate. One mirror per node (symmetric with per-system router); receiving mirror owns fetch/restore after the authenticated head notice. The mirror is the psyche's cross-machine self: editing on any owner machine propagates as a criome-authorized state transition; criome holds authority on the latest approved head and Spirit fetches it. The mirror aspect may fold into the Spirit daemon.

Specialized lanes are made explicit via skill files and orchestrate role discipline, and agents may create/adopt new lanes (e.g. nota-designer) when a topic demands it. cluster-operator handles live maintenance and production lojix. cloud-designer/cloud-operator are cloud-scoped lanes with their own report subdirs and orchestrate-cli claim support. system-designer handles system topics (CriomOS, horizon, lojix, goldragon, deployment), renamed from system-assistant; system-specialist is renamed system-operator. videographer is a new main role for video as craft (capture, editing, captioning, encoding, publishing-prep), paralleling poet.
The orchestrate component must be kept on the current triad, signal, sema, and runtime crate set; updating the component dependency surface, not compatibility-helper lockfile work, is the path.

In schema, camelCase names are fields (instances); PascalCase declares or references a TYPE. Field names DERIVE by lowercasing the type name — an entry is just a list of type names (Entry (Topic Kind Summary ...)). A PascalCase type may be declared inline at first use, reused by name; resolution is declare-before-use, single-pass. Top-level namespace entry makes a type export-visible. Divergent field names use a newtype (Certainty (Magnitude)); the bare name-type form declares a distinct newtype, aliases lose it.
Spirit record identifiers must not be reused after removal; reuse makes references unstable because a later record can occupy the same identifier.

Beauty must prevail as a top-priority filter in workspace design and code. Aesthetic quality — terseness symmetry schema-driven self-describing interfaces-first composable-boundaries — is a gate alongside correctness not a nice-to-have. Audits apply the beauty filter as primary lens. Workspace beauty extends beyond code to capture discipline (avoid duplicate intent records) report retention (avoid proliferation without retirement) and substrate cleanliness (no hand-written code where schema can drive).
Register maintainer lane with reports/maintainer/ and orchestrate-cli claim support. The lane inherits system-maintainer discipline and is scoped to active troubleshooting and host maintenance.

Newly-designed components must actually drive the live system end to end — the three engines, trait-defined ordering, typestate mail, origin route are the real execution drivers, not dead scaffolding beside an older code path. Verify in implementation that every designed component is on the live path.
Constraints are among the most important parts of architecture: express design as constraints when manifesting it into architecture, because a stated constraint becomes a test that verifies it.

Spirit's intent gate / guardian runs a strong (open-weight if self-hosted, provider TBD) model — intent is too important to under-resource. Relevance, duplication, contradiction are model-only semantic judgments, so the judge sees the full evidence bundle; relevance is computed by a model once at write time and stored as a referent for cheap scoped reuse, bounded by referent/domain scoping and corpus agglomeration.
Designer's deliverable is intent / architecture / skills, not reports

Mobile Android clients on the Criome WiFi access point should get near-native name resolution for cluster services; improve the AP-to-Android resolving path before falling back to ordinary public DNS names.

Agents differ from runtime functions by persistence and identity, not capability: long-lived agents (designer, operator, poet) hold cryptographic identity and accumulate signed work, opinions, reputation; runtime functions are stateless per-call LLM invocations with full context and no durable identity. Intent-capture/extraction (per audio span, may rephrase STT and route prompt sections to agents) is such a function.

Every actor has its own schema (a channel-contract) declaring base enums: ACTION (what it can do) and RESPONSE (what it can say), plus a universal Unknown response for unrecognized requests. Execution matches schema variants to select replies and route work. Execution is fan-out: one interaction-actor decision emits multiple parallel outputs (effects, replies, messages to other actors), closed per interact-trait variant.

triad-runtime intent must not describe Tokio listener task shells as actor-native actors. Only real Kameo actor nouns get actor wording; listener loops that run as Tokio tasks should be described as async/task-backed shells with actor-backed admission when they use RequestGate.

Most correct wins, not shortest or smallest. When choosing between implementations — unifying duplicate engines, or reviewing one implementation against another — pick the most correct: better logic, better logical planes, better names. Fewest lines is not the criterion. *refactoring*

Agents using Spirit must track the DEPLOYED version's wire interface, not current persona-spirit source: the deployed pinning is reachable through the CriomOS Home flake input — use Nix metadata commands (flake metadata, derivation show, path-info) to find the pinned commit, then read the wire contract at that revision. Operator changes to persona-spirit and its signal contracts drift main from production until the next CriomOS rebuild.

Owner-socket peer-credential authentication via triad-runtime ConnectionContext SO_PEERCRED REJECTS the connection on a uid mismatch and fails closed for the privileged owner meta tier Deploy Pin Unpin Retire. Defense in depth complementing rather than replacing the socket file-mode guard.
For nix-managed Emacs configs, produce .eln native-compiled artefacts at nix build time as part of the derivation; runtime JIT native-compilation invalidates on every nix rebuild and is forbidden.

Horizon and its cluster-data are minimal on two axes. At the semantic boundary Horizon expresses only WHAT the psyche-as-cluster-user wants — simple typed facts, never HOW; Nix consumes them and owns all composition and decision-complexity. At the type-count level reuse the input type as the output type rather than parallel in/out types. Upstream principle for the Horizon rewrite.

Pipe-brace traits and impls are a data catalog referencing impls/methods already on the Rust side, so schema can name them and a method-calling syntax invokes them from schema bodies. An impl fuses onto its target type: a type declaration carries a trailing pipe-brace impl block, target implicit. Each entry is a bare trait atom (marker), a trait atom plus bracketed method signatures (body-bearing impl), or a bare signature (inherent method); a signature is name + brace parameter struct + return type, naming an existing impl. Calls resolve from receiver shape plus this catalog.

The CriomOS VM-testing node feature carries a per-node gpu-passthrough option (VFIO GPU passthrough, used for the gamma visual test). It is DISABLED on the Prometheus node — Prometheus is an AI node whose powerful GPU must not be monopolized by vfio-pci passthrough; Prometheus runs the non-gamma harness and the routed test VM, while the gamma passthrough test runs on a node where passthrough is acceptable. Extends records 2630, 2631, 2632. *virtualization*

persona-spirit is the production Spirit component (its own repo, currently the only component in production); the repo named spirit (formerly spirit-next) is the next-generation schema-derived Spirit — the active schema-derived implementation should carry the spirit name, since the separate spirit-next repo only arose from the psyche referring to the next Spirit. Future Spirit expansion uses spirit-next as design inspiration while tying together the richer data in production Spirit. Schema work has its own repo named schema. The -next replacement repos have won: nota-next is the canonical NOTA and schema-next + schema-rust-next the canonical schema stack, deprecating the old nota/schema repos; the now-vestigial -next suffix should be dropped, renaming replacements to base names.

Canonical prose spells criome for the authentication component and criomos for the operating system; on-disk path/code-identifier spellings are preserved when cited. The psyche-facing approval component is mentci (repos mentci-egui, mentci-lib); the earlier Menchie spelling and the Menchie referent were a speech-to-text error to migrate to mentci.

Sub-agent sessions land in a meta-report directory: reports/<role>/<N>-<name>/ with the orchestrating agent's final report and each sub-agent's report numbered inside; the directory IS the meta-report and is garbage-collectable as one session unit
For stack parts being replaced by a new repository, use a next suffix when the new repository is an upgrade of the previous concept rather than a newly named concept.

persona-prefix retirement and owner->core terminology: persona-spirit becomes spirit, signal-persona-spirit becomes signal-spirit, owner-signal-persona-spirit becomes core-signal-spirit; the pattern drops the persona prefix where not needed and moves owner terminology to core terminology because core is the privileged control/library layer while signal-* stays the ordinary messaging layer for safer callers/extensions. signal-persona-auth renames to signal-persona-origin.

Agents register every worktree they create in the orchestrate tool, which keeps a database of the active worktrees, and follow a standard worktree lifecycle protocol: each prior worktree is either merged to main, archived (recorded in a manifest that drives later garbage collection of the branch and tree), or recycled (rebased on main and reused for new work). This tools the kb4k worktree-tracking-and-archive principle and the oust feature-branch-worktree convention with concrete orchestrate-tool registration plus an archive manifest.

The canonical stored Spirit record stays one uniform shape — no per-kind storage variants. A view of a record or a creation shorthand would be a presentation/ergonomics layer over the uniform record, not the stored shape; whether such a view is useful is undecided.

Component triad split: client-facing Signal types live in the signal-<component> contract repo (canonical; daemon imports them); Nexus and SEMA interfaces live in the daemon repo as runtime-internal. SEMA may extract to a contract repo when a daemon gains a scale-out database. Cross-schema imports reference another schema's locally-defined namespace types, leaving room for a future shared schema library; per-schema self-definition suffices for MVP. The schema type/macro-substrate library may co-locate in the triad or sit in a dedicated schema-types repo consuming the same compiled NOTA artifact — only crate-layout differs.

NOTA has two bracket-string forms: [content] inline-bracket-string for single-line strings; [|content\|] block-string for multi-line strings and for strings safely containing single [ or ] without escaping (delimited [| / \|] so content may include [, ], or newlines freely). Both are distinct NOTA shapes the shape-logic layer distinguishes via is_block_string and the underlying parser.

NOTA classification is 'qualifies as', not 'is': a token qualifies-as-a-symbol if symbol-safe (PascalCase/camelCase/identifier alphabet); whether it ACTUALLY is a symbol is a type-level question deferred to the macro/schema layer. At parse time promote to the higher classification (qualified-symbol over string) since demoting later under type context is easy while promoting is hard; the schema layer demotes to string-only when its types require.

Component binary naming: each component (role name, e.g. persona, spirit, harness, orchestrator) has a CLI half <component> and a daemon half <component>-daemon — the role name is not itself a binary. Persona is composed of binary 'persona' (thin CLI) and 'persona-daemon' (long-lived daemon); converge older phrasings ('persona engine', 'engine-manager daemon') to 'Persona'. Persona disambiguation suffixes only when wrapping (persona-codex, persona-pi, persona-claude). In the running-orchestrated-system goal, Orchestrate is the orchestrate COMPONENT (repo /git/github.com/LiGoldragon/orchestrate), the runtime that runs the set of component daemons (persona the supervisor, the introspector, the schema daemon, others) — not the act of orchestrating.

Agent-authored content is not psyche-authorized design surface: distinguish psyche-authorized direction (cite the intent record) from agent-drifted content. Retracting agent-drift restores reality rather than invalidating decided design, so framing such retraction as invalidating substantial design surface mis-attributes authority to agent output.

the Macro declaration form is the locked NOTA syntax for declaring user-defined macros inside a namespace: positional record of the form (Macro Input+ Output) - Macro tag plus one or more input shapes plus exactly one output shape. This sits inside the namespace alongside ordinary types per record 843; the macro lives as a registry entry resolvable by qualified name. Per Q2 of designer-assistant 372 the explicit Macro tag wins over the contextual shape-driven matching alternative for introspectability.

Help retrieval is always one NOTA argument: every component supports (Help) / (Help Main) / (Help Verb) and nested forms like (Record (Slot1 (Workspace Help))) in its NOTA vocabulary, never bare-word CLI args. Help docs likely auto-wired via the signal-channel macro.
Every code or logic change to a component bumps its version (at minimum the patch, e.g. 0.3.0 to 0.3.1) as part of the same change, so changes stay trackable across the fast-moving stack.

When the existing shape does not fit, follow where the logic wants to go rather than being bound by what is already there — let the water find where it wants to go downhill. Desire paths name this: the trail walked into the grass because that is how the body naturally moves, regardless of where the paved walkway was put. Architecture grows the same way, and where multiple agents independently arrive at the same shape, that convergence is the signal it is the right shape.

ARCA performs database migration whenever an ARCA-touched format changes. Every component that stores derived types in ARCA migrates its own database when the relevant format changes; the cascade is mandatory. No format-change escapes downstream migration.

Component state across the Criome stack is last-known-acknowledgment, not live-query. Components with a relationship of state constantly agree via natural acknowledgment, forming a quorum-of-agreement. This is the root pattern at Criome — components share state by always agreeing whenever it changes. External non-Criome systems (current cloud providers) break this; until they speak the protocol, the local state is the last acknowledgment we received from them. Future federation will enable cross-stack last-known-state status with non-Criome providers. *replication*
Spirit's overriding design goal is to be maximally clutter-free, a curated pristine intent store rather than a capture-everything log

Psyche/operator/designer reports demonstrate working systems with real artifacts run end to end, not marketing or line-count summaries: show actual code lines with surrounding context, and authored schema input plus generated output plus the runtime implementation each shape creates (this-code-creates-this-code), and the primordial typed text forms (NOTA, schema source). Re-ground from first principles; separate implemented from design-only; explain opaque items plainly. Use short focused graphs split across parts, each visual paired with real code. Macro examples are large working examples from current tests showing structural and named @ positions.

The entire built-in type-name vocabulary lives in Schema, not NOTA: scalars String, Integer, Boolean, Path and composites Vec, Optional, Map, Set, Box are Schema's type-reference vocabulary, alongside struct/enum declarations. NOTA stays pure structure (its delimiters) plus the codec and value literals None/(Some x). The (Vec X) tagged FORM and value-vs-type distinction hold ([X] is a vector value, (Vec X) the type) but live at the Schema layer; the codec stays in nota-next as serialization. Refines record 1137.

Psyche design decisions apply to the WHOLE design unless explicitly scoped, not just the local prompt example. When absorbing any psyche design correction, ask 'where else does this rule apply structurally?' before stopping at the surface where the example was given.
Actions are taken, not announced: instead of writing ready-to-close/merge/ship status phrasing in chat, the agent either does the action itself or asks a specific question naming the choice.

Workspace component deployments must come from main-backed inputs unless the psyche explicitly authorizes a non-main deployment. Operator integration work lands feature-branch substance onto main before deployment.

Spirit profile defaults must not cut over to a new versioned database until existing intent records are migrated or otherwise reachable from the default command; a side-by-side new daemon with an empty database is acceptable only as an explicit test surface, not as the unsuffixed production surface.
Designer lanes are different windows into the same persona; share context through workspace files plus per-session work summaries

The cluster Rust toolchain is the newest nightly, pinned durably through fenix's flake lock (bump fenix to advance Rust cluster-wide) rather than a rolling stable channel guarded by per-crate frozen sha256. Per-crate flakes route the toolchain through shared rust-build, whose fromToolchainFile yields fenix complete nightly (all components, no hash); a crate gets nightly by repinning rust-build.

The daemon concurrency primitive — the bounded thread-per-connection worker model that lets a daemon serve many connections at once without blocking on a long operation — belongs in the REUSABLE shared runtime triad-runtime, not duplicated per component. Every triad daemon (lojix, cloud, future components) reuses the same bounded-worker primitive from triad-runtime rather than reimplementing connection concurrency. Concurrency of connection serving is a runtime-kernel concern, captured once as a shared component and consumed by each daemon. Refines 2alg.

Schema reader is multi-pass NOTA-first: one universal NOTA parser yields a generic NotaValue tree, context-sensitive macro passes interpret each value per its NodeDefinitionPoint position, then assembly produces canonical schema. Parsing decomposes by object via positional source spans; each block carries shape predicates (bracket/paren/brace kind, root-object count, recursive nesting checks) and each root object is itself a typed positioned block, so recursive parsing is a chain of block-level queries. Delimiter roles: parens=enum/variant, square=vector/token-vector, braces=name-value namespace map. Pass 1 finds identifier uses+locations; pass 2 registers inline declarations and resolves references, supporting bare-PascalCase-or-Variant-Payload shorthand, with source representation deciding define/reference/both. NotaBodyDecode is the semantic entry: document/block decode strip wrapper context and delegate to the body decoder, which reads the inner object stream per the expected type (struct/vector/enum/value). Schema has its own typed source-node model (structural-macro nodes, namespaces, paths, fields, variants, references) before assembly and acts as its own codec so shorthand round-trips. Block reassembly concatenates parsed blocks in order as first-class units.

Spirit CLI invocations default to inline NOTA wrapped in shell double quotes; the bracket-string notation keeps NOTA itself double-quote-free so the whole object passes as one shell argument. Temp-file NOTA paths are reserved for genuine need (binary signal-encoded paths, or shell metacharacters too painful to escape) — multi-line context fields are not such a case.

The schema language is self-describing: the meta-schema is defined in the schema language's own format and the language is specified by its own .schema file. A schema-library repo holds a bare schema file defining the foundational namespace (Schema, Channel, Operation, Reply, Event, Namespace, TypeName, TypeDeclaration, Path) and bootstraps via its own basic struct format. Schema needs explicit specs for AssembledSchema types and the minimal built-in object, plus a self-description tested as an artifact that lowers through the current engine and exposes the root Schema shape and nested declaration types — even while the bootstrap Rust reader stays hand-written.

Work orders are not intent records. Operator work starts from current intent, designer reports, and actionable beads; schema MVP work leans from durable intent, not only from discovered implementation gaps.

The per-role psyche-to-agent flow is the role's protocol (designing, operational, poet): when the psyche talks to a role, that conversation designs that role's operating skill. The role label in the window determines which protocol the psyche speaks through; psyche conforms to that interface.
Persona runs as a permissioned system daemon (privileged, supervising component daemons)

Value lean, efficient design and do not fear breaking things. Breakage belongs in development, not production; breaking freely in development is what development is for. Lean means no duplication and no boilerplate, not the fewest lines.
Spirit operations should report outcomes with self-describing NOTA enums and structs instead of long free-text status messages wherever the state can be represented as typed data. *error handling*

Greenfield non-production new-stack rewrite repos (lojix, signal-lojix, meta-signal-lojix and peers) push straight to main with no next or feature-branch ceremony; the designer-next/operator-main split governs only the production code repos. The rewrite work must live in version control and be pushed, not in untracked trees.

When the psyche signals they have lost the thread or no longer understand the design, stop advancing the work and re-ground them top-down: explain the system being touched from first principles in plain language, then iteratively the systems within it, checking understanding at each layer. Keeping the psyche able to understand and respond is a precondition for proceeding, not an afterthought; losing them in implementation detail is a failure to actively avoid.

inline NOTA in any single-line string literal context - Rust string, shell argument, markdown inline - must NOT contain backslash-n escape sequences; NOTA is whitespace-insensitive so newlines add nothing semantically; spaces between tokens are sufficient; the backslash-n form is a hybrid that pretends to be multi-line while being a single string and is ugly to read; for multi-line NOTA use ACTUAL newlines in authored files like dot-nota dot-schema files, or use multi-line string literals like raw strings or concat! macros, but never escape-newline-in-single-line; this rule covers test fixtures, inline shell calls, code examples in docs and skills, and reports; update skills/nota-design.md and any docs that demonstrate the wrong pattern

Current-state reports live in the working tree; deleted/superseded reports live in commit history and are retrieved with jj show <change>:path or git show <commit>:path. This is why supersession-deletes-in-the-same-commit is safe.

Owner/permission/unpermission distinction can live as a data-type Permission variant (Owner/Permission/Unpermission) inside the signal, with a general socket routing by variant prefix. Current implementation keeps the two-socket filesystem-managed approach (ordinary socket + owner socket, access controlled by filesystem) — that stays; the macro implements as-if-two-sockets so a message is authenticated by the socket it arrived on. Permission-in-signal-variant is deferred until needed; development mode runs as local user trusting itself.

Canonical three-part vocabulary: SCHEMA is the specification part. SIGNAL is the movement (transport). SEMA is the body (data at rest, state). Schema specifies both signal and sema; signal carries movement across channels; sema holds the body at rest. This closes the loop between specification, transport, and state.

When a data-carrying variant's payload is a single-use enum, inline it as a bracket list at the variant position — (Busy [DatabaseOverloaded ResourceDisconnected OtherReason]) — rather than naming an enum and wrapping. Name it separately when reused, semantically independent, large (10+ variants), or part of a stable cross-component contract.

Routing intent through a third-party hosted inference API is acceptable and is not a privacy violation; the privacy-closed-by-default rule guards against publication to world-readable surfaces such as public repos, which a hosted inference call is not

Schema file root identity comes from filename context — Spirit schema file is implicitly named spirit because the filename is spirit.schema. The file body is the contents of a Schema struct; the root name does NOT appear inside the file. The file is read like a path knowing we are reading a struct of type Schema — self-embedded logic. Each schema is a root enum named after its component.

Each typed symbol (type, variant, field, operation, route) has a fully-qualified SymbolPath identity through the interface's global namespace — the workspace's canonical machine-readable universal symbol form, not a per-design convention. Machines reason about identity via the qualified path; NOTA renders the same path as human-readable text at user-facing edges.

Version magnitude follows the digits: a small change bumps the patch digit (0.3.0→0.3.1), a functionality change such as privacy work bumps the minor (0.3→0.4). Any component code or logic change bumps the version at least at patch level so deployed binaries and reports never carry stale version numbers after behavioral changes.

Cross-host component transport for the version-control mirror is a tailnet-bound TCP listener in triad-runtime, reusing the length-prefixed frame codec, with peer identity as a typed closed sum distinguishing kernel-vouched Unix-socket peers from tailnet TCP peers. Ssh-forwarded sockets are rejected as the transport shape.

NOTA is at heart a hack on the text user interface — using common sense, good patterns, beauty, delimiters, and typed structure. NOTA is a typed language; everything is read as a known type in data-type-theory terms. Structure macro nodes can have structurally different shapes at certain positions because they extend the type-declaration vocabulary, but the macro extension is itself typed, so the node still resolves to a known type. A well-formatted valid NOTA expression decodes reliably to its declared types. This is the deeper rationale for the workspace's NOTA-everywhere direction — typed-text reliability through a beautiful user-facing surface.

Persistent memory for tool-using agents is the queryable tool-call trace, not the model context window. Expose query_history back to the agent — structured filter, summary-on-demand, or vector retrieval modes. This is an MCP-first architectural property: structured tool protocols give perfect recall by construction; UI-driving agents do not. Applies to any tool-using agent: video editing, browser-use, deploy automation.

Gap-fill from forwarded designer exchange confirmed by psyche: NOTA owns raw structure and serialization shapes, plus the value literal None with present values as (Some x). Schema owns the entire type-name vocabulary: scalars such as String, Integer, Boolean, and future scalar names, and composites such as Vec, Optional, Map, and future composite names. Moving only composites to Schema while leaving scalar type names in NOTA is inconsistent; all type-name keywords belong to Schema.

Migration is a logged fold: replay the previous store's versioned operation log through the version From-chain into a store at the next schema, recording a typed migration entry — retiring copy-everything binaries. Per version-boundary hand-written Rust bridges handle moved/transformed types; auto-migration runs once on DB load when the version marker lags, then updates and persists it. Hash-identity migration builds a hash-to-former-ordinal mapping table while live lookup moves to hash identity.

Second-designer (and parallel-lane designers) act as counter-ego to the prime designer — auditor and critic of their output, not merely parallel-capacity workers. The counter-ego role is to doubt, challenge, identify drift, surface contradictions, and catch flaws the prime designer might miss in their own work.
Nix workspace integration builds and flake input overrides must use pushed remote refs only; local path flake refs such as path:/git/... and git+file:// are forbidden in commands and Nix input code.

The Spirit short identifier (returned by the tool and cited in chat) is the shortest-unique base36-lowercase prefix with no collision, minimum FOUR characters (about four to seven), not three — refining the prior minimum-three scheme.

An agent is a machine; it does not misbehave and there is no such thing as a violation. An agent's output is a function of its context and prompt — when an output looks wrong the question is which part of the context produced it, not which rule was broken. Every agent failure is therefore signal that the guidance is insufficient, and the fix is to upgrade the guidance so it absorbs the failure mode, never to blame the agent.

Before dispatching a new subagent the designer first researches what other agents have done since the last pass and what is in flight, to avoid duplicate work and stale-substrate assumptions. Operators read peer operator reports first and watch new designer reports.

Lane management moves from the transitional bash tool tools/orchestrate to persona-orchestrate (the typed component), with the lane registry and claim/lock surface in scope; lane registration and lock acquisition become first-class operations on its working signal. The discipline (role) field is an open string-shaped identifier, so new roles are added via one owner-channel operation without contract bumps.

Build an always-on real-time intent-recording system on Prometheus using a real-time multimodal model (latest Gemma): the recorder always runs so the model carries full surrounding context, and spoken keyword markers ('Beginning of intent' / 'End of intent' and variants) delimit which spans to extract as discrete intent entries. Achievable on current models at negligible compute; triggered by losing an unrecorded spoken design session.

Authorize migration to the schema-driven Spirit stack (spirit-next consuming nota-next + schema-next + schema-rust-next). The stack follows the signal/executor/SEMA runtime triad, the methods-on-schema-objects discipline, the build-time emission flow, and the layered architecture from /361 + /367. Migration is now the work-direction; production v0.3 spirit retires when spirit-next reaches feature parity. Operator owns the migration cadence; designer track contributes verification + comparison reports. Per psyche 2026-05-26.

Privileged and root-level operations on cluster hosts are performed by SSH-ing into the host root account authenticated with the operator SSH key, for example ssh root@localhost or ssh root@ouranos.goldragon.criome and the same ssh-root form for any other host. This is the standing privileged-access mechanism across the workspace, and sudo is not the access path.

Schema names carry meaning from their definition site and refer to typed declarations elsewhere in the schema or imported from another schema, never free symbols invented at use sites; references may be forward. The namespace is dependency-ordered (basic definitions first, derived later; consumers reference only earlier-declared names) and assembled schema makes names globally unambiguous by context. Imported schema names must not collide in one context.
Harness-private session-scoped tools (TaskCreate etc.) are organisational scratchpad only; substance must land in workspace files before session ends

The workspace ships a library of precompiled schemas. A standard namespace - the core - is always implicitly loaded; it contains the macros and built-in types. Other schemas load as needed. Precompilation means schemas dont re-parse at every interpretation site; they live as in-memory namespace tables. The library is the precompiled-schemas surface that emitters and interpreters consume. *runtime environments*

Syncthing is excluded from the phone media mirror path because it is too heavy for the phone; Immich remains the phone uploader/gallery candidate and any raw-file surface must avoid adding Syncthing to Android.

schema-cc is the schema compiler-compiler: the schema language and compiler definition (reference grammar, dispatch precedence, built-in heads, shape vocabulary, emission rules) is kept as typed data that GENERATES the schema compiler (schema-next and schema-rust-next) rather than being hand-written, bottoming out in the nota-next seed. Push as much of the compiler definition into data as possible.

lojix uses the two-contract authority split: Deploy/Pin/Unpin/Retire are owner-only policy in meta-signal-lojix (a deploy mutates the live cluster and can break the router, the strongest owner-socket case); Query, the WatchDeployments/WatchCacheRetention subscriptions, and Unwatch are peer-callable in the ordinary signal-lojix. The policy contract is born meta-signal-lojix, never owner-signal-lojix. Until cutover carry meta-signal-lojix as a local path-dependency package inside the lojix tree (mirroring the cloud stopgap); create the standalone repo at cutover.

Anything with a deterministic correct answer derivable from its input — routing, dispatch, lookup, classification, projection, address resolution — is mechanism, not agent work, and must live in code or schema-derived machinery, never in agent judgment. Agents are the cognitive layer reserved for decisions code cannot make. The trajectory: more agents doing more substantive cognition, less mechanical work per agent as the system matures. This is the load-bearing why behind the schema-derivation/macro-emission thrust and the small-model auditor.

Spirit verbal depth scope vocabulary settled — Shallow Recent Deep VeryDeep with target counts 5 15 30 100 respectively; bare Recent adopts the explicit 15 count for consistency with the verbal-scope family; the three new variants append at end of RecordedTimeSelection per the record 1249 rkyv discriminant stability rule

Every PascalCase name in the schema header (Input/Output root enum bodies, NexusWork, NexusAction, SemaWriteInput, SemaReadInput) becomes an exported top-level type importable by library consumers. The header IS the component's public type-level interface: each variant is a named, defined, exported thing — the contract of verbs and replies the component supports.

Crate and module names must honestly describe what the thing is; a name that abbreviates a capability the thing lacks (naming a crate 'auth' when it is not an authentication library, the signal-persona-auth case) is wrong.

Because agents currently lack a reliable source of intent and forget what matters most, which is the biggest gap in LLM-based development right now, the priority is to get Spirit and one or two components working excellently before spreading effort across a larger working area
Schema enum variants are themselves enums or data-carrying enums — mandatory two-layer enum-of-enums structure

Before creating the next schema-stack version, operators and designers study each other's latest work first: designers rebase prototype worktrees from operator main, and operators integrate valid fresh designer intent into main.

Built-in core macros live in the basic schema library and are ALWAYS imported when a schema imports the schema module - no opt-in. They define how to assemble data structures (enum / struct / newtype / etc). User-defined macros are lazy-loaded by name from explicit imports. The split: core macros are non-negotiable substrate; user macros are extensions that compose with the core.

Schema files are strict typed component interface contracts: their input and output roots plus imports and namespace define the messaging/API surface the engine implementation must use, forcing agents and generated code to communicate through schema-emitted objects rather than ad hoc messages.

near-term production deploy target for spirit criome-gating is the simplest 1-of-1 local authorization: spirit asks its co-resident local criome daemon to authorize the content-addressed head, and a single local signature, 1-of-1 with no quorum and no multi-machine cluster, suffices to gate propagation. This is the m0p2 bootstrap case promoted to the first production milestone, letting the spirit to criome to router to mirror chain from d6he ship to production without waiting on the full criome cluster; quorum authorization and the multi-machine cluster from jk1w are the subsequent step, not a prerequisite.

The prime designer chair runs parallel subagent workflows by default (a designer-specific exception to the no-subagents default still binding operator, system, poet, and assistant lanes); it does not extend to second/assistant designer lanes unless the psyche says so. The composite designer runs Claude and Codex in parallel on the same prompt, then selects or merges. Operators use parallel subagents only when authorized, staying responsible for integration, constraints, tests, and the final report.

Context maintenance is research-driven refresh, not deletion: read intent first to weight currency, ask how each stale report relates to engine/architecture/intent now, re-research and write a superseding 'review' report bringing substance forward. Output is a small load-bearing set of current-state reports, not an accumulating archive.

Agents must not share one jj working copy while concurrently advancing the shared main bookmark — that forks commits off-main and clobbers uncommitted files between write and commit. The workspace needs per-agent jj workspaces (separate working copies via jj workspace add) and single-writer-to-main discipline so only an integrator advances main, including for reports.

Every message carries an ORIGIN ROUTE as automatic metadata that is NOT declared in the schema - it is implicit on every message - acting as a return address that travels the whole way through signal then nexus then sema and back, so a reply is associated with the originating query when it returns. The origin route is internal to each component, a short statistically-unique identifier with effectively no clashes; it need not be a long hash, it is just a return address echoed back. Refines the message-identity intent records 930 960 by making the correlation identity an implicit round-trip return-address metadata field rather than a schema-declared field. *remote procedure call*

Domain subdomains are mandatory all the way to a leaf, so a domain value is always complete. DomainScope is not Domain with optional payloads; it is a separate generated prefix language over the same recursive domain tree, a ScopeOf construct, where stopping early is the prefix semantics rather than optional subdomain data. Every scope segment is a typed enum variant and the notation stays clean with no Some or None exposed; the generator owns the internal representation

Domain relations live as first-class, highly visible schema-declared vocabulary in a dedicated taxonomy schema surface, a new schema interface kind alongside the plane schemas, not an ad-hoc data file. The relation and expansion mechanism is reusable across components such as the mind.

Daemon has three execution centers whose traits encode order: Signal (messaging; first gate, rejects invalid messages back to client), Nexus (mail keeper + translator; holds mail being-processed, converts Signal<->SEMA), SEMA (state). Push only on success; push methods take the next engine as a parameter, sequencing Signal->Nexus->SEMA then reversing. Nexus may host an internal recursive engine for future runtime control.

The schema-derived runtime supports an optional testing/instrumentation build surface where generated objects and engine interface usage emit structured trace events to a logging socket, proving actual runtime use of Signal, Nexus, and SEMA interfaces during tests rather than mere symbol existence.

Communicate is the wire trait between any two components, over binary rkyv with signal-frame (connection setup, async unique IDs for request-reply correlation, handshake). A universal mail-queue manager commits intent on accept; the reply carries a database marker (hash plus counter) so clients verify the transaction and guarantee local-state consistency against the daemon's authoritative database.

A trace-client library in the repo owns the display plus SEMA-log features; the CLI is a thin wrapper enabling and calling those features rather than reimplementing listener/decoder logic per component. The generic CLI trace-siting path lives as a triad-runtime helper, not one-off schema-rust-next emitter glue.

persona-llm-client becomes workspace-native component `agent` (drop persona- prefix), its own triad — agent CLI + agent-daemon + signal-agent + owner-signal-agent — a supervised component others call for pre-configured API agent calls (not a thin wrapper, not subsumed into Pi). Hooks to mind for skills/context (model call + agent = a thinking process); takes typed NOTA directly from the model via prompt examples + validate-and-retry, grammar-constrained when self-hosted.

Private component state is organized as multiple key-gated stores on the GoPass model: each store is encrypted to a set of recipient keys, and access is key possession, so the per-store key is both the access boundary and the crypto-shred erasure unit. Accountable content-erasure via crypto-shredding with a signed erasure receipt is acceptable. Explore rethinking Spirit as a federation of key-scoped stores that multiple Spirit instances can share as ciphertext, with queries scoped to the stores whose keys the instance holds. Answers the granularity question of report 95/11; refines 29pb, j487, i4ak, x0ja.

Forge is the emerging build-system family, eventual Nix replacement: forge-nix-builder first wraps Nix and extracts as a library under the existing forge daemon (still the Criome-stack executor), not replacing it. Forge-core is the shared standardization contract sub-forges adopt as they mature. Keep what is eternal in Nix (content-addressing, derivation graphs, hermetic builds); move auth to Criome and binary signing to a workspace content-addressed store. Generated Rust may later become content-addressed crates directly, removing generated-code repos. Phase Nix out as forge matures.

Privacy classification: infrastructure/general-knowledge/agent-training/component-design intent stays privacy Zero (public, helps all agents); personal substance (logistics, business, family, finances, health, location, plans) gets elevated Spirit privacy — Low for mildly personal, higher when the psyche frames it sensitive. The Zero default fits the public-repo development-mode data shape and could reconsider in private-journal mode; private repositories remain home for substance kept out of Spirit entirely.

The structural patterns of the schema substrate (deep typed trees, closed variants, recursive composition, separation of syntax/verification/semantics) emerged FIRST in the psyche's Aski language work — aski-core (rkyv contract types for the askic-veric-semac triad: compiler, verifier, semantics), vscode-aski tooling, and the core parse-tree primitives (body, domain, expression, module, pattern, primitive, program, statement, trait, type). The schema engine is the second iteration of these insights. (Corrects a prior STT artifact mishearing Aski as ASCII.)

Local AI on Prometheus: install both big Gemma multimodal models — ~31B dense (max quality) and ~26B MoE (a4b, ~4B active/token, fast) — at bf16 with f16 vision projector, and deploy multiple quantization variants as separate selectable models in the llama-server router, each named by quant in modelId (e.g. gemma-4-26b-a4b-bf16), sharing one mmproj per base model so users pick quality vs speed. Local Pi and agent sessions select these as a local provider. Prefer one curated best/latest model per capability over near-duplicates (prefetch large candidates freely; models need not run simultaneously). Video speech-to-text is tiered: best path on the large-AI node, smaller local STT for weaker machines.

Introduce an archive system as a specialized sema-database holding exactly one kind of archived object, the database name stating what it archives, paired with a small archive-retrieving tool. When composite intent retires its source records, the sources are archived and referenced by hash identity from the composite so provenance survives cleanly. CollectRemovalCandidates archives collected records automatically to a default archive surface before hot-store removal, with typed retrieval rather than ad-hoc caller files.

The CLI is the log surface: in testing mode the log socket routes back to the CLI, which displays all engine logs over the same wire substrate as production interaction — no separate logging daemon or sink. Testing-mode logging is configured by typed NOTA. Schema-emitted objects carry optional-compilable feature-gated logging hooks at the macro/emitter layer (off in production, on in testing) that log object usage through that same socket.
Components treat recompiling to change an enum set as trivial; zero-downtime upgrade is a design goal, so schema and enum changes are not a cost to design around

Prometheus gets a sturdy backup admin network from attached USB devices: one USB wifi adapter serves a backup access point (SSID criome-backup, human-readable random password in sops-nix), two USB ethernet adapters form a master router interface. It runs at system level on plain systemd-networkd with simple DHCP and forwarded DNS, independent of the main kea/dnsmasq/hostapd stack so a switchover cannot take it down. Backup devices stay optional — their absence must not fail router networking, and devices plugged in after boot are configured when they appear.
Schema-defined execution consults current state and trusted owner/core authority messages before mutating storage; the single-owner model remains the race-avoidance discipline. *consensus*

A content/data daemon's privilege boundary is can-ingest-and-serve-content, not can-read-anything-on-the-system: it is fairly privileged (ingesting/serving content is not especially dangerous) but never absolutely privileged. Its store stays unwritable by anything except the daemon itself — even root writing it is system misbehavior. It has no ambient access to system private-key material and, handed a path to a private key, must be unable to read it.
The categorisation of error kinds into subtypes develops over time, and that subtype development is one of the most important parts of designing the Criome stack. *error handling*

Long-lived agents have cryptographic identity tied to their Criome master public key (ephemeral keypair when Criome is absent, transitionally). The public key IS the agent identifier — no separate UID storage; graduated short forms (~3/5/7 chars) are methods derived from the key. Possessing identity is the power to sign opinions; Criome Identity is a SignalCore primitive and the canonical existence-verification surface.

Spirit exposes a single guarded CollectRemovalCandidates Signal-root operation that collects all Zero-certainty records (the supersession removal marker), archives them, then retracts them in one call with archive-first safety — if archiving fails nothing is retracted and affected candidates return archive-failed. The discovery-versus-destruction separation is preserved inside the one operation (archive preceding retract), matching deployed behavior and o70j which archives before removal.

Short-header structure: a 64-bit micro-message header (1 root enum + 7 sub-enums) discriminates any namespace object in constant time, emitted by every signal contract macro. Byte 0 is the ROOT VERB (Help/Query/Message); bytes 1-7 are verb-namespace sub-variants. In schema the header is a positional [Variant ...] vector; data-carrying variants (paren name+data) take slots 0-6, unit variants (bare PascalCase) follow, so adding a unit variant is a no-op wire upgrade. Root variants limited, nested ones broad. Sub-enums default to one byte but pack tighter by inner type (Bool=1 bit, Option=2, 16-variant enum=4 bits), letting SEMA beat raw rkyv; freed bits encode deeper nesting and multi-byte sub-enums cover >256 variants within 64 bits. Integers are typed values, not sub-enum kinds. Three sizing tiers: (1) 64-bit micro header, (2) 64-byte summary message (hand-built from natural summary fields, basis for a subscription tier), (3) full unrestricted rkyv message.

Macros are sugar in the schema layer: the brace-enum form {Variant Payload ...} expands to the canonical paren-list ((Variant Payload) ...) — both lower to the same AssembledSchema, where variants are already (VariantName payload-option) records in a homogeneous vector. The macro engine dispatches on multiple criteria — delimiter, internal shape, root-object count, symbol qualification, and combinations — choosing the most specific. Enum-body vectors honor homogeneity: each element is one variant declaration — a bare PascalCase symbol (unit) or a parenthesised (Name Type) record (data); the older interleaved at-suffix VariantName@ PayloadType is retired as dishonest. The macro shape itself is two-position: pattern it matches, then template for how it expands.

Schema-next keeps one lowering engine — the most correct lowering path, not dual paths or the smallest patch — and schema-derived Rust emission targets that engine rather than rewiring into the old signal macro implementation.

Schema diffs drive per-type upgrade trait requirements, compile-time-optionally per main-next pair: unchanged types emit no code, changed types need hand-written upgrade behavior, database load runs needed upgrades, old-version messages are upgraded/accepted/logged. Upgrade knowledge belongs to next, which declares the next schema crate as a Cargo dependency so the macro sees both schemas and emits the VersionProjection.

The NOTA schema namespace section is a key-value MAP {key value ...} of user-defined types — keys are type names, values are definitions — not a flat sequence of separate declarations. Inline enum: EnumName (Variant1 Variant2 ...), parens holding variant names, variants themselves possibly nested enums. Structs are a different syntactic shape. Universal Unknown injection stays a behind-the-scenes macro mechanism; no user-authored Features section.

The schema-rust-next emitter consolidates same-shape sibling variants by semantic family: when variants in a closed-sum enum carry identical payload shapes, it groups them under one family-named variant whose body carries the original variant name as data (the common case for rkyv-wrapped closed sums).

Every root enum emitted by schema-rust-next gets an auto-generated Help action variant sourced from the schema interface description; help responses are themselves generated root-shape enums carrying their own Help variant (recursive). Self-description is part of every component's typed interface contract, projected from schema, not a separate doc surface.

Rust emission goal: a single macro in lib.rs/main.rs reads the repo's .schema files and generates ALL schema-derived Rust — data types plus NOTA and rkyv read/write — as the sole derivation entry point. The signal-to-sema engine (Spirit full-schema branch) carries per-turn query and effect-to-response schemas, participating in this same path.

The primary workspace has a new system-maintainer role: the system maintainer owns mostly updating, debugging, and deploying Crayon OS on various hosts, is expert in Crayon OS and Logic, knows the current production and development stacks, overlaps system-operator but is less development-focused and more maintenance/deployment-focused.

Each agent has its own Criome identity created at agent start and recorded in persona-mind; its Criome public key is the authoritative authentication anchor. Complex (especially agent-initiated) Criome interactions run through the agent's own Criome daemon; authorization for many workspace operations checks against Criome, not SSH, and any component/daemon can escalate to Criome for a check. Substrate is per-component-per-domain agent spaces. Criome is per-Unix-user: each user runs their own home Criome daemon with own key custody, the Unix-user boundary being the trust boundary — no shared multi-user system daemon. The privileged system Criome tier serves host-scoped system services under a service user that a home Criome peers with, not a shared daemon; the same single-user custody boundary rules out an embeddable Criome engine library.

message and router stay separate (the SO_PEERCRED trust boundary cannot move into router). message owns the EXISTENCE fact — an authenticated message authored and witnessed at the SO_PEERCRED ingress — as a durable event it emits. router is authoritative for delivery on the routed gated-or-remote path (durable on the harness channel ack). Direct-delivery fast path: addressing a publicly-reachable local agent by uid delivers peer-to-peer without router, establishing delivery on the target's direct ack.

Bird's persona gets a local operator-controlled OS update path on Zeus. For Crayon OS host maintenance the maintainer has root SSH on all cluster hosts but cannot SSH as Bird on Zeus, so Bird's Zeus home-profile redeploy uses an existing root or maintainer-user path rather than direct Bird SSH.

The Playwright Chrome browser-extension token lives in gopass at chrome-browser/playwright-mcp-extension-token, and browser automation wrappers should read that entry at runtime without printing the token.

Schema source carries the triad engine mechanism as baseline — runner shape, trace plumbing, continuation substrate by generation. Authors implement the three plane engines plus effect handler and budget-exhausted reply; the generator bundles those as glue for the shared runner. Generated traits carry minimal lifecycle hooks (on_start/on_stop); the emitter evolves to host lojix load-bearing daemon properties (bounded per-request workers over a shared store, typed owner meta tier, frame/timeout hardening). Per-component variation uses explicit escape hatches.

Wire-header pattern grows by extension not replacement: the 8-byte Tier 1 micro-type header is the base and lives as a parseable prefix inside longer (256/512/variable) headers holding most of the frame structure. A header declares an enum identifier plus ordered endpoint nodes and encodes enough top-level variant shape for cheap routing; schema declares which fields fit in 8 bytes vs extended positions, with data vectors following as header-indexed memory arrays. Every message carries the 64-bit micro-type marker (type, size, root structure), rkyv zero-copy after a network-fingerprint check, the prefix doubling as a default-on tap-anywhere observation channel. An extended 512/64-byte Tier 2 sits between the micro variant and the full rkyv record for payloads needing public keys, identities, signatures, or short descriptions (e.g. a Criome quorum authorization payload).

Persona Pi is the Codex-advisor harness for Persona, target shape a Codex replacement, shipping packaged Pi extensions. Default config is GPT's latest strong model at maximum/xhigh thinking out of the box (currently GPT-5.5 xhigh, GPT-5.4-mini selectable, roles may override). Spawn is tested against Ghostty / terminal-cell spawning.

Schema variant namespaces reserve explicit numeric ranges so the encoded form reflects the logical split before deeper payload parsing. Input and Output are partitions of one wire tag space (e.g. a 1-byte ~240-slot enum split roughly two-thirds: ~120 input then ~120 output slots — illustrative, larger enums get larger spaces): the author declares Input and Output separately at the schema level but rkyv merges them into one tag space, so a single tag byte identifies both the variant and its direction, the basis for input/output dispatch at the message layer.

AssembledSchema distinguishes exported public data types from local module data types — modeled either as visibility on declarations or as public/local declaration variants — and the compiled Rust preserves the boundary.

The agent component is a new abstraction over harness backends — persona-claude/codex/gemini/pi/open-code are its backends — built as an LLM-API-call component making provider HTTP API calls in API style, not an agent-harness front door (harness backends deferred); Router talks to the agent component, not harness directly. DeepSeek-as-library folds into the persona-pi triad (no separate deepseek component). Persona Pi is a real persona-component triad (daemon + signal-persona-pi + meta/owner-signal-persona-pi + thin pi CLI), running inside a terminal-cell for Unix I/O but also talking directly to the harness API (the more accessible logic surface). Mencie, the persona multi-modal UI, is implemented as nexus schemas, one per UI panel.

rkyv's storage layout reserves more namespace than data needs (a bool occupies a byte; a small enum's discriminator fits in a byte). This headroom enables zero-cost migration-free schema changes: adding a unit variant that still fits the byte, appending struct fields under append-only encoding, widening fixed-width ints, provided variant order is preserved. The schema-layout schema exposes this headroom so derivation can skip zero-cost changes. Cap'n-Proto-style structural-compatibility discipline lives at the rkyv layer, not the NOTA text layer.

Schema-stack presentations, tests, and design reports show the schema-to-interface path: each component interface, the schema that creates it, the path to generated interface and component communication, plus the derived REST code, typed interfaces, traits, impls, and derivation boundaries where flaws can be spotted.

A schema root header is a bare list of PascalCase names for Input and Output roots — each name is both the operation/reply vocabulary and an exported top-level type that library consumers import and that resolves to a real named type defined in the namespace or inline. Headers list exported variant object names directly with payload shape resolved from the namespace, rather than repeating each variant's payload as a parenthesized pair. The header declaration is a vector of ordered enum variants / namespace nodes, not one payload pair per verb.

Runner concurrency mode is a runtime/deployment configuration choice; the public contract does not encode how parallel a daemon runs. Schema declares semantic constraints (ordering, idempotence, cancellability, read fanout, single-writer) only when those semantics are real.

Spirit-next production-candidate testing must include a production-copy handover test: seed a production-like SEMA database, copy it, start or exercise the candidate runtime against the copy, verify existing state is preserved, and verify candidate writes affect only the copy, not the original. *integration testing*

Integration tests exercise the actual execution chain through trait surfaces with per-plane schema-typed values throughout: real .schema files drive Nix codegen; the Nix-built CLI and daemon launch; the CLI sends real rkyv signal frames over a real Unix socket; signal-engine (Signal->Signal), nexus-engine (Nexus->Nexus plus translations), and sema-engine (SEMA->SEMA durable state) process and reply end-to-end. Never bypass the trait surface or collapse typed events to tokens; assert Signal/Nexus/SEMA boundaries were entered through engine methods at runtime, on typed schema-emitted outputs.

Lowered output uses fully-qualified universal identifiers (crate::module::type) so cross-component collisions are impossible and the form self-documents for verification and machines; the sugar form is for authoring, both equivalent. Imports clashing with a local name error rather than shadow. Schema and Rust mirror by naming, so a colon-path like spirit-next:signal:Frame greps from either side.

Generated Rust is one module per .schema file, mirroring schema layout on disk so name clashes namespace per module. Separator is a single colon mirroring crate:module:type (crate name from Cargo). Each crate has a schema folder with lib.schema entry; emission targets src/schema/ beside hand-written Rust, re-emitting automatically with watch-hook hot-reload; placement stays pragmatic, interfaces schema-derived.

The signal/sema macro derives the typed wire layer from one schema source: signal_channel! embeds the 64-bit Tier-1 micro header by default (LogVariant + frame_micro projection), standardizes the two-enum-namespace shape (ordinary + owner contracts sharing byte-0 namespace via golden-ratio split), derives the small object directly (no hand-impl for Tier 1), gives every enum a Help variant documenting its namespace (command docs hook into Help), emits NOTA encode/decode from the same source, and emits a VersionProjection between current and next schema versions as the upgrade path.

Move schema declaration out of Rust macros into a specialized NOTA-format schema language that is the SOURCE of truth: codegen produces (a) Rust types/impls, (b) per-version namespace-slot-assignment tables, (c) version-diff auto-marking types for upgrade-migration on recompile. The Rust macro becomes a consumer of structured schema data, not a parser of Rust syntax; compilation auto-assigns slot numbers and size optimization. NOTA signal schemas are also documented as a class (the wire vocabulary in NOTA form) separately from the binary signal layer. Direction firm; exact shape needs iterative design.

Workspace-wide version vocabulary: NEXT = the in-progress version being authored (authors always write from next's view); MAIN = the current published canonical baseline, imported as comparison point; PREVIOUS/LAST = the prior iteration of any contract, schema, message format, or data shape. If main == next nothing changed and no upgrade path compiles; if main != next the machinery generates the From-chain from main to next.

The cloud flake wraps flarectl with a gopass-backed shim that fetches the Cloudflare API token from the password manager at path cloudflare.com/api-token and exports it as CF_API_TOKEN before exec. Realises the FEMOS env-var-populated-by-password-manager auth pattern end-to-end inside the nix closure, with the domain-style Cloudflare credential path as the canonical provider token handle.

persona-spirit is the deployed production Spirit; spirit-next is the schema-derived pilot. The production component carries a clear prominent in-code production marker so agents patch the right repository while two stacks coexist. Legacy persona-spirit daemon slots remaining in the side-by-side deployed set are needed and should be fixed to run.

Identifier design treats the shortest reliable identifier as a first-class target: opaque content-addressed hashes (blake3/sha) tokenize near one token per character, a recurring cost in agent context, logs, and reports. The full digest stays canonical identity; the exposed locator is a stable shortened form, never renamed once exposed.

Telos = umbrella meta-project (Greek: ultimate end), far horizon eventual Criome + Sema, realized now as Persona (meta-AI of LLMs over typed dumb mechanism). criome is Telos's agreement-and-authorization organ (universal agreement machine for authorization), not Telos. Quorum is criome's universal primitive: one membership-scoped quorum serves both reliability/credible-time (a principal's own multi-node) and cross-party trust (multi-principal). criome's internal language is a limited typed policy language over public-key identity atoms (not a general VM; discipline from Ethereum/Tezos/Solana VMs), composing identity contracts from k-of-n signature quorums and time-locks with time-varying thresholds plus explicit divergence-reconciliation objects (conflict resolution may invoke LLM-oracle). Verdicts come from gathered BLS quorum signatures, a connected approver over criome's meta socket (mentci is that client), or configured auto-approve policy (bootstrap/testing); contracts escalate to a named adjudicator (mechanical quorum, LLM panel, smarter agent, or psyche) whose signed content-addressed verdict criome only verifies. Verdicts are closed typed outcomes (approve/reject/defer/non-judge/escalate) over already-submitted objects, never carry new content; non-judgment is first-class, default LLM judge leans abstain+escalate, psyche is highest-authority lowest-availability (EscalateToPsyche inert dead-letter until psyche UI exists). criome may run as multiple deployment instances (system + home/user), same two contracts differing by privilege tier. domain-criome is the Criome domain component, excludes provider APIs and direct CLI store access. signal-standard is the second shared signal- library (alongside signal-frame): ComponentKind roster+differentiator (closed-but-partitioned), then standard typed connection points (Unix socket path, optional port).

Upgrade mechanisms are live typed SEMA operations on the Asschema: a protocol or database-format change is a typed operation/message, applied as database work and used as source for derived datatype and upgrade/compatibility code. The same library handles record-level and schema-changing operations. Diff operations have three families: Add, Remove, Modify (Modify subdivides into ContainerEmbed, EnumWrap, Reorder, KeyChange).

Type end-to-end, never string-keyed: extend the typed model first (e.g. add a real NodeService enum variant with NotaEncode/Decode in the horizon model), author the fact, project typed, consume typed. Typed-record vocabulary = trees of branches (variable-size/data-carrying: vectors, data-carrying enum variants, structs with variable members) and leaves (fixed-size terminals like unit-variant enums). SymbolPath is a flat vector of Name segments recovering role from schema position. A pure-projection library (Horizon, no runtime triad) needs a types-only-module schema variant so datatypes generate without forcing an Input/Output signal plane. SignalCore holds workspace-universal core types in the 64-bit namespace partition; each component contributes its own zone.

Signal-frame protocol (framing, short-header dispatch, route-enum derivation, codec object) belongs declaratively in a root-level signal-frame.schema imported by component schemas, which inherit frame methods on their Input/Output surfaces — schema-as-protocol-substrate replacing hand-rolled transport route tables and frame codecs. The Spirit full-schema branch keeps public and permissioned sockets as separate signal schemas (equivalent to current ordinary/owner contracts) generated from schema files rather than the legacy signal macro surface.

Horizon splits into RAW horizon (core typed cluster-data model: NodeService enum, typed facts, arch resolution, name/key-line newtypes, cross-node fan-in, typed validation, secret-binding resolution) and a separate PRETTY horizon layer that computes pre-derived variables in typed Rust (not Nix): behaves-as/gating booleans, resolved magnitudes, lid-switch policy, trust-gated extra-groups; Nix consumes only already-derived variables. Split is also a forward-integration seam: raw stays clean+minimal to later promote into a real component, pretty stays the typed derive layer. Applies ONLY to the new lojix/horizon-next stack; production Stack A horizon stays as-is until cutover. Spend the cleanliness budget on the durable layer that outlives the bootstrap substrate (Nix, replaced by future Forge); unavoidable glue ugliness lives in the throwaway substrate.

Generated and handwritten Rust prefers real data-bearing types and trait/associated-type bindings over transparent aliases. Bare namespace bindings (Rejected SignalRejection) lower to TypeDeclaration::Alias (pub type), not newtypes; genuine/unnamed single-field bodies stay transparent newtypes (only multi-field structs get field names). Generated APIs spare callers nested wrapper construction, which signals a missing emission layer.

The introspection component is named introspect (no persona prefix) and uses schema-next triad engine interfaces; it serves as a configurable trace destination for all components, deciding what and how to log, and provides a queriable source of tracing-derived intelligence.

Each engine trait's method count matches the number of distinct wire events that plane handles: SignalEngine has two (triage on input arrival, reply on output emission), NexusEngine has one (execute, a synchronous compute step), SemaEngine has two (apply for writes, observe for parallel reads). The spec follows method-count-matches-wire-events.

The schema declares an EFFECT TABLE: a closed mapping from message types to effect types, with replies declared per message. Dispatch is structural match through the interact-trait contact point (message -> mapped effect -> mapped reply); effects, messaging, and interactions are all mapped, and internal work composes them. Match always; map always; match rather than compute.

Nexus output is slim: input is a query/decision, output a result/what-happened acknowledgement. Clients issue follow-up queries for specific state rather than receiving everything; the wire contract stays compact and identifier-shaped, with detail living in the engine.

Persona daemon schema migration uses in-process versioned reads: every record carries a schema-version tag, the daemon links every prior version's types and dispatches read-side on the tag, migrating older records on read (optionally rewriting), zero downtime per bump, applied to every triad. The per-type Migration trait carries a self-check/peer-coordination message knowing whether to consult the next-version daemon over an explicit version-handover protocol. Pilot record-shape changes are clean-break; production migration is operator work at cutover.

Once persona-mind ships, agent errors (mermaid syntax, NOTA formatting, naming violations, etc.) are logged into Mind as typed events, forming the basis for skill-improvement loops and auditor input. *error handling*

Each persona contract carries an explicit content-addressable schema-layout schema in a NOTA-based language: its hash is its identity, so any edit changes the address (the version). The version-checking pipeline detects schema-address mismatches between code and stored data, walks the diff, and derives per-type migration operations (zero-cost variant extensions, append-only fields, structural migrations) baked into how types are written. MigrationIndex is the runtime decoder lookup; migration is per type; upgrade and downgrade are one compatibility-projection relation.

Browser control is three-tier: a cloud orchestrator (any agent, e.g. GPT-5.5) instructs a local actor model (e.g. Gemma 4 Flash) on the CriomOS AI node, which reads the browser visually via Chrome DevTools Protocol and acts; the CLI's model calls go to that local node, not an external API. The CLI runs from outside the cloud daemon as a packaged PATH command (no orchestrator triad/RemoteBrowse op); a Playwright MCP path is optional. Use supervised scout mode (scan, report state and next steps, await human decisions before consequential actions) on the human's main Chrome profile when safe.

The schema component is self-editing: the schema daemon IS the editor of its Asschema, receiving upgrade messages and deriving both new data-type code and upgrade-migration code. It has three faces from one NOTA source: runtime daemon (serves header-to-schema lookups, resolves schemas/namespaces/core definitions/macro libraries with in-memory caching), library crate (schema types other crates import), and macro substrate (consumed by signal- and sema-side macros at build time).
Spirit without a configured guardian fails closed rather than accepting ungated writes. *admission control*

When you see repetition, extract it into a library or the schema macro; prefer pushing logic back into the schema macro. The more the schema generates the better, as long as it stays good — schema is clear, readable, and shows the logic as object shapes, which is cognitively sensible.

Spirit operations support a variant ladder: short forms with summary defaults for common operations, complex forms with full metadata for precise control. Both coexist as distinct wire operation roots; the complex root is canonical and the short root expands to a default of it.
A NOTA formatter can be derived from the macro definitions, since each macro's pattern tells the formatter where line breaks, indentation, and spacing belong.
