The three NOTA delimiters map to the three schema sections globally: parentheses hold enum/variant headers and choices (generic enum fields and header root endpoints included), square brackets hold positional struct field vectors/bodies, and curly braces hold the name-value namespace of type names to definitions.

Two trace forms are supported: COMPACT carries only the root variant name, and EXTENDED appends the nested variant chain when a variant payload is itself an enum (stopping at the root when the payload is a struct), with the enum-vs-struct distinction known to the macro at compile time.

Schema header variant references resolve via namespace lookup or inline declarations: a bare PascalCase name in Input/Output root bodies looks up its namespace type, an inline declaration at variant position auto-registers it, and root bodies take inline payloads directly (e.g. [Record { Topic * }]). Multi-pass resolution replaces the verbose (Variant Payload) form.

Trace events may be persisted to a SEMA database purpose-built for trace storage, as a client-side sink alongside NOTA display; the daemon emits typed binary trace frames regardless, and clients choose display-to-stream or persist-to-sema.

The Spirit RecordQuery composes filter dimensions: TopicSelection (multi-topic, partial=any-of or full=all-of, plus topic-catalog queries), optional Kind, CertaintySelection (filter by certainty/magnitude to select e.g. removal candidates), and a first-class RecencySelection (Any, Newest N, Since identifier/timestamp, Window range). Also support numeric-identifier lookup (exact and ranges), qualitative depth requests (deeper/very-deep) instead of exact counts, public-only/private-only shortcut verbs, and an explicit privacy-query subtype exposing at-most/equal/at-least selectors while normal queries stay Zero-only.

The cloud daemon starts almost-stateless and caches last-known-state of queried provider (Cloudflare) resources; cache loss is acceptable because the provider is the source of truth. The first cache is runtime/volatile (in-memory), provider-backed and possibly lossy; persistent storage is deferred until state worth preserving emerges.

The .schema source uses header-first sections with no outer wrapper, and is not a flat vector of same-kind declarations. Schema root input and output are known positional fields serialized as positional input/output enum bodies, not data-carrying variants labeled (Input ...) / (Output ...).

Trace enablement is controlled and documented per crate/component, since interfaces may enable or suppress tracing independently; the tracing interface itself stays untraced for now so the trace system does not recursively trace its own events.

Most workspace logic is MATCHES between two domains: domain A enum times domain B enum, the logic being the match-matrix outcome per cell. Cells with no defined behavior return a typed error or help (Unavailable etc) — the error surface IS part of the trait surface. Engine operations expose these tree-to-tree and enum-to-enum matching surfaces.

Spirit-facing skills (skills/spirit-cli.md canonical, intent-log.md points there) document the CLI thoroughly so agents don't scramble: invocation (inline NOTA vs file-path arg), every operation (Record, State, Observe, Watch, Unwatch), the current production Spirit version and record shape plus the deployed wire shape and how to find it when source drifts, error replies, environment variables, and observability — so agents don't call stale versioned wrappers.

Editor selection is data, not hardcoded: Horizon user-preference data carries a typed preferred-editor setting per user, and Home/profile configuration consumes that preference instead of hardcoding editor choice downstream (concrete per-user choices live in code/cluster data, not intent-record descriptions). For now, if VSCodium is enabled in a CriomOS-home profile it is the default editor for file-opening links and editor MIME handling, including Markdown and source files such as Rust.

LojixOS split is a rename-and-configuration split: generic OS substrate becomes LojixOS; CriomOS becomes a simple per-deploy configuration crate holding a thin NOTA data tree that lojix precompiles into binary config. Cloud-node OS images live in CriomOS as a minimal CloudNode-species profile, declaratively built with an image builder (not by snapshotting a converted running droplet) and content-sized to boot fast on the smallest sensible droplet (multi-tens-of-GB unacceptable). Per-node configuration is declared in the cluster data (horizon cluster proposal). Cloud daemon references the image by id via HostPlan image_name and never holds the image definition; CriomOS owns the image definition + provider-format build, cloud owns snapshot-id selection and provisioning.

When the psyche says 'signal' (the noun, on its own), the term is ambiguous: it may refer to the rkyv-encoded binary signal traveling on a socket or to the NOTA-encoded text signal that agents read and write. Disambiguation is required when context does not pin the form. Psyche proposed the term 'nota signal' for the text form (softly: 'I guess we could call it'); the term is therefore proposed but not yet hardened into workspace vocabulary.

Chat replies referencing a report must include the report PATH (reports/<role>/NNN-slug.md) as the locator, not just commit hash or report number — the path lets the psyche open it directly. Applies to all report-referencing chat and audit replies.

Schema/module-system shape: braces are structurally key-value maps; namespace is conceptually dynamic-enum-like; fully qualified schema names use a single-colon namespace separator; crates carry schema/lib.schema entrypoints and schema modules; generated Rust emits into a schema module tree.

For the CriomOS-home dictation/desktop-audio path, keep a reliable laptop-microphone fallback and avoid a hard DJI hot-loop dependency: prefer the connected DJI Bluetooth microphone through system audio default-source policy rather than a Whisrs-specific wrapper, and reduce/delete brittle DJI/PipeWire policy rather than adding keepalive code. A DJI keep-alive may exist only as a sidecar consumer of the real DJI mic source, keeping it awake without becoming the selected source; do not let the fix grow into special-case code.
Single-type field syntax may be (Type) for an existing defined type, and a single-name enum variant record carries the same named type.
Ouranos swap and zram configuration is expressed through cluster data and Horizon projection rather than a host-local CriomOS override.
A registered but undelegated domain name returns a typed no-records result.

Lock selectively: claim only the files or subfolders you will edit, never the whole workspace (over-locking drives branch-dancing on primary; reports are claim-exempt). Claims start from a clean checkout. Agents track every worktree they create and clean it up once the work is captured into a report; periodic best-ideas passes archive worktree-branch ideas, then dismantle the trees.

The schema type system reduces to one canonical kind plus the unit case: everything is a STRUCT (data-carrying) or a UNIT VARIANT. Preferred shape is enum or vector-of-enum-variants; a Vector is structurally an enum (choice of all its members), a Vec a single-field struct. Maps are reserved for namespaces. Enum data-carrying variants ARE structs; variants are unit (bare PascalCase) or data-carrying (parenthesized), positional, in order. Bool's True/False is a two-unit-variant enum — why NOTA capitalizes booleans.

Composite type constructors are NOTA-layer datatype objects that schema reads, not schema-only sugar: the collection macros are Vec, Option, and KeyValue (not Map — risks reading as a verb — nor KeyValueMap; KeyValue is followed by key type then value type). Vector sugar is structurally a single-field container object. NOTA does not own type declarations; schema does — plain NOTA / type-reference positions can name scalars or composites (String, Vec) without declaring a new type, while declarations use positional struct, enum, and newtype forms.

An interface is a root enum with more than one variant; a root naming only one operation is an incomplete design and a newtype, not an interface. Input and Output are the two primary interface roots; payloads are structs (leaf data) or enums (nested interfaces), and interface chain depth measures design realism.

Add an auditor role that closes the loop back to designer — mostly mechanical (doubter that finds flaws, bad patterns, broken workspace rules; knows the rules). Automate it; a smaller rules-and-flaws model (DeepSeek) is the main auditor going forward.

lojix-daemon owns GitHub-authenticated Nix flake input resolution (the GitHub API rate-limit stale-activation incident is a deploy-path problem owned by lojix, not a Pi-package problem; resolves system-designer/31 OQ 7.2 toward lojix-daemon scope over skill-level discipline). Mechanism: an authenticated wrapper around the Nix invocation fetching the user GitHub API key from gopass and injecting it into the Nix call (likely via NIX_CONFIG access-tokens). Form: a small Rust library lojix-daemon calls that encapsulates secret-fetch + auth-injection so the rest of lojix-daemon never handles the token directly.

Root signal shapes should begin with an enum differentiating input and output spaces: reserve variant-number ranges over an 8-bit root enum so one first-byte header distinguishes request-like input variants from response-like output variants for the same actor mechanism, keeping dispatch cheap.

Each of the three schema types (Signal, Nexus, SEMA) is its own LANGUAGE sharing the SAME 4-position shape — Imports, Input, Output, Namespace — and the SAME import/export mechanism: a single-colon namespace path mirroring Rust modules (one colon, not two), e.g. signal-frame:Frame or schema:spirit:Entry. The languages differ only in which types and macros fit each position. The namespace position is both export and definitions surface; imports/exports are part of the root structure with conflict checking across imported exports; selective object import and load-all are both supported. Names must resolve from defined namespaces or imports; for the MVP, Spirit may define all types locally and imports may be deferred.

Schema supports inline declaration and file-path references (paths resolved into the full spec before macro processing). A lowering executor recursively walks the NOTA schema tree dispatching per node by bracket/paren shape, lowering transient sugar into canonical form: bare identifier = reference to a namespace entry (resolved by lookup); square-bracket vector = enum declaration with variants; single-paren single-identifier = newtype tuple wrapping the named type; multi-element parens = struct with positional fields; (fieldname [variants]) = inline named enum field. Schema node values lower by context; bare identifiers resolve to namespace objects.

Schema-defined type trees are normally 6-7 layers deep: enum-of-struct-of-enum-of-struct-of-enum-of-struct etc. The same types are referenced in multiple places throughout the tree via a TYPE INDEX (a flat index of named types that the tree refers into). Deep nesting is the normal shape, not an exception. The schema engine must traverse and emit through arbitrary depth via canonical recursive walks; no ad-hoc per-layer handling. Cross-reference resolution goes through the type index so the same type appearing in many places resolves to one canonical declaration.

Spirit record identity uses a random/opaque hash, not a content-address fingerprint: because records mutate, content-addressing adds computation and little value while implying stable-content semantics that no longer hold.

Spirit archive semantics (meta-signal Configure ArchiveTarget) are redirect-forward-only: setting an archive target re-points future archive writes and reads to the new target; records already in the prior archive database stay in place and are NOT migrated forward. Confirms report 541 open call — archive is a redirect, not a migrate-forward operation.

The four-character bracket-pipe delimiter is for pretty indented multiline string blocks with common indentation stripped on parse, not the ordinary inline delimiter. Keep one pipe-text shape and escape literal close-marker text rather than widening fences.

At a slot whose type is already known to be an enum, the authored value may omit only that enum type name and provide the enum body directly. Thus ((Parse Expression) (Render Expression)) is valid when the surrounding slot supplies the outer enum type; the fully named equivalent would be (SomeEnum ((Parse Expression) (Render Expression))). Only the known outer type name is omitted; variant tags remain named inside the body.
Spirit random identifier codes should use four characters as the minimum displayed/queryable code length, aligning with Beads practice.
The DJI microphone keepalive path should keep the microphone hot for speech-to-text so recording does not lose the first two or three seconds to Bluetooth wake/profile churn. *instrumentation*

Use plain English for schema root terminology: the authored schema root should be described as a struct with fields named input and output, not as abstract surfaces unless that term is specifically needed. Avoid invented vocabulary when ordinary field/struct language says the thing directly.

When an interaction needs the engines state including outbound queries like Criome authorization the interaction becomes ASYNC. The actor system handles the wait; other code (other actors interactions) continues running while the query is outstanding. State access is implicit not explicit - the interacting object queries state through the engine without the caller threading it. Async-by-default makes the interaction composable with the rest of the actor world. *asynchrony*

When psyche questions are open, designer infers a lean from past psyche patterns and presents the uncertainty rather than freezing. Designer may act on high-ratification-probability recommendations (lossless, no-downtime, cheaper-and-simpler, mechanical renames) without explicit approval, capturing the ratification via Spirit Decision; it holds back on competing-without-lean or proposed-not-decided items.

Spirit needs a real in-place record mutation path so an existing intent record's content is edited rather than removed-and-recreated. Production Zero-certainty review is not deletion; the system exposes an explicit garbage-collection path for removing reviewed Zero-certainty records. Hard deletion is inevitable on a long timeline because storage is finite, so the design treats hard deletion as a deliberate planned decision rather than a forbidden state.

The schema-derived stack (nota-next, schema-next, schema-rust-next, signal-frame.schema, spirit.schema) does not reference Nexus, a separate NOTA-using vocabulary track (nexus + nexus-cli). Reserved sigils ~@!?*= are reserved at the NOTA syntax layer but the schema stack does not consume them; schema macros are plain NOTA records dispatched by position+shape in the schema-next MacroRegistry, not sigils.

Cloud-hosted compute nodes are assigned low cluster trust (Magnitude Min), making a cloud node a real but minimally-trusted member, because it runs on third-party provider hardware outside the operator's physical control. Minimal trust keeps a cloud node out of the fully-trusted builder, dispatcher, cache, and admin-key roles, which are all gated on Max trust, while it still joins the cluster as an ordinary client. doris, the DigitalOcean CloudNode, is set to trust Min on this basis.

An agent educates itself in a domain before submitting intent into it: query and read the domain's existing intent (the domain and the broader domains above it, plus records sharing the candidate's referents) before recording, proposing, clarifying, or superseding, so most submissions resolve to a duplicate, a merge, or a clarify rather than a guardian refusal. The guardian is the fallback, not the agent's first read.
The schema-derived stack uses separate repositories for nota-next, schema-next, and schema-rust-next rather than one combined integration repository.

Schema files form namespaces (prefix initially the file name) mirroring Rust-style module decomposition: qualified colon-separated names or names split across module files, resolved via typed import variants (all, from path, from repo root, adjacent directory, git; namespace imports with optional selection) and re-exports, never flat-global. Entries can reference other entries, enabling recursive types and meta-schema self-bootstrap.
Synthetic bare-metal firmware-gating work stays generic, avoids unrelated unfree firmware, and is verified with constrained Nix checks.

Plane payload types shed their plane prefix: the per-plane envelope (Signal, Nexus, or Sema) carries the plane, so payloads are named just Input and Output, scoped to plane modules (rename SemaInput/SemaOutput/NexusInput/NexusOutput to plane-module Input/Output), naming the plane once. Because a plain vector denotes homogeneous elements, the assembled root must not show differently-typed Input and Output as bare sibling vector elements — it uses a true common wrapper or a struct/field shape preserving their distinct types.

Daemons should mostly contain component logic and algorithms that engines run; repetitive startup, runner, and transport boilerplate belongs behind libraries or macros. Generated internal Nexus-plane nouns are not casually promoted to the daemon crate root or public contract surface — they belong in the plane schema/module, and future crate boundaries keep internal Nexus vocabulary separate from wire-facing signal APIs. The VersionProjection home crate is named version-projection, peer to signal-sema.

mentci is a first-class component triad and a state-bearing programmable user-interface daemon owning canonical interface state (UI changes because daemon state changed); TUI, CLI, editor, status-bar, popup, email, and agentic clients subscribe and send updates back. It interacts heavily with the local criome for question/approval flows and key decrypt/use (criome owns the key store). Shape: mentci daemon repo (daemon + thin CLI + daemon-local signal/nexus/sema runtime schemas) plus signal-mentci and meta-signal-mentci wire contracts; mentci-lib is the shared app/state-machine library reused by daemon and clients, and mentci-egui is one thin client.

Help is a noun (the documentation entity itself), not a verb: bare (Help) is top-level/channel-level help; walking the path to Help retrieves the documentation entity for that node. Help/documentation is schema data in a mirror description namespace over the global symbol namespace, with generated defaults when no explicit description entry exists for a fully qualified symbol.

Spirit must handle concurrent capture of the same psyche prompt across multiple agent windows: take a lock so two agents do not both record the same intent; Spirit capture calls become asynchronous and non-blocking so a blocked agent is not stalled and later receives a response naming which records were accepted or refused during the lock and why; the agent can then recognize the duplicate came from the same prompt and either accept it or make its case for a better wording. This requires agents to record their own originating prompt so Spirit can deduplicate captures by prompt. Operator implements a lock for now.

Spirit defines a small-record type carrying core load-bearing fields — identifier, topics, kind, description summary, magnitude, daemon-stamped date/time — distinct from the full record's metadata. Short-form reads (Recent, Today) and CollectRemovalCandidates emit it; archiving and downstream tools consume it. The observation mode emitting these fields should be named to match its data (DescriptionOnly is misleading). Reply schemas avoid nested summary-shaped acknowledgements, and outcomes (archive-created, archive-appended, backup-archive-used) report as typed variants, not string messages.

Common workspace identifiers (record, forward, send, etc.) are stored as enum-encoded composite names; the wire form is the discriminator, not the string, so the namespace is composable across components and enables multilingual labels.

Spirit provides RecordDefault, a short-form recording op taking only commonly-customized fields (topics, kind, description, magnitude) with defaults injected for the rest (privacy Zero/open per dev-mode public-repo grounding, daemon-stamped date/time). Record stays the canonical full-fidelity op; RecordDefault is the daily-use shortcut.

Rename away unnecessary persona ancestry for Spirit-facing repositories and surfaces: Spirit should stand as the component name, signal-spirit as its ordinary signal layer, and owner terminology should move toward core terminology for the privileged/core surface.

Schema changes that affect stored data should be accepted through an upgrade testing pipeline: derive the new code, start a newly compiled daemon, test against a minimal specified database first, then test against a disposable copy of the live database before accepting the schema change.

Schema headers are ordered enum-root declarations (a vector of ordered enum variants), not namespace vectors. Assembled root syntax carries Input and Output bodies directly in their known positions or via a real common enum object, never wrapped in meaningless one-field structs added to satisfy vector syntax.

Schema namespace additions follow append-only discipline (new names added at the end of the available namespace) and enum slot planning minimizes database rewrites, so future enum-like compilation and upgrade logic stays compatible across upgrades.

criome's clock is decentralized quorum-attested coarse crystallized-PAST time (no single trusted/precise clock; only the past is provable). A time attestation begins as a forward-extending window proposition (content-addressed as its identity); a quorum signs that the present falls within it, closing at last signature or expiry, crystallizing a non-forgeable monotonic lower bound on now. Window extends forward so every member can sign; tolerance gap bounded below by quorum signing latency, widening under partition. Every quorum-signed object carries an AttestedMoment (a signature is never timeless), so every admission envelope, verdict, agreement fact, and triad input/output is time-bound for freshness/replay; time-locks compare against the operation's own proof-of-when. AttestedMoment is the self-grounding base case carrying no inner moment, stopping the regress.
Schema files use dot-schema extension

The persona system is federated at its core: each persona triad (mind + orchestrator + spirit) is one federated unit. Orchestrators across personas and namespaces become aware of subtasks they send, interfacing via skilled-agent subtasks; each persona offers itself as a context-bearing thing whose opinion can be queried, making federation natural rather than special-cased.
Concept designer is an ephemeral occasional invocation, not a persistent named lane
The `[| |]` block string form is useful as a safe wrapper for multiline NOTA-like content that does not contain the block delimiter itself.
Rename only after confirming there are no external source dependents. *refactoring*

The engine-manager daemon's canonical short name is Persona (the entity name; engine-management is the role) — repo persona (lowercase), binary persona-daemon; the longer 'Persona engine' is acceptable but Persona is preferred. The whole workspace stack is the Criome stack; 'Persona engine' is the more specific term for its AI-work part.

Streaming is full schema-derived push (not poll-as-permanent, not lojix-only): teach schema-next an event/stream root with opens/belongs relations; teach schema-rust-next to emit the event frame into signal-frame's StreamingFrameBody + ObservableSet pub-sub; add push action and subscriber registry to triad-runtime. signal-lojix carries WatchDeployments/WatchCacheRetention from day one; until push lands, handshake+poll suffices. SubscribePolicy default TerminateAtHandover: subscriptions end at handover, clients reconnect to next.

Schema files carry structure, not explanatory comments — declarations are positional and self-explanatory through the type names they declare; explanatory prose lives in the Rust implementation files.
Schema diffs can infer standard migrations, but ambiguous transforms need explicit annotations or traits.

Schema namespaces use maps reserved for true name-value namespaces; keys and fields provide names, and namespace order supports linear loading. Schema grows nested type namespaces mirroring Rust modules (e.g. Envelope.Destination) so role-local names exist without polluting one global namespace, with serialized references using a string-level delimiter (e.g. colon). Namespaces can import local type namespaces from other schemas; imports use explicit import variants (not an opaque Path-with-optional-selection), and the import section is a key map whose values are import variants. Other extensible shape favors enums and vectors.
Skills should instruct agents to use the unsuffixed spirit CLI for normal intent capture; versioned Spirit wrappers are diagnostic or explicit testing surfaces, not the normal agent command.

Design analysis targets the ideal: for each thing, work out the best, most correct, future-oriented, reusable pattern it should be in an ideal world, and contrast it explicitly with what exists now so the gap is visible. The ideal pattern, not the current shape, is the design target.
During multi-component migrations, agents should claim and lock one component at a time rather than locking the full component fleet, so other lanes can keep working.

The schema daemon resolves and caches schemas in memory and owns the namespace surface; agents and code emitters query it to resolve namespace references, so all cross-schema resolution flows through it. It is the runtime arm of the precompiled-schemas library.

persona-introspect is the home for cross-version error logs (full failed messages in version-encoded format for later recovery); Pi library integration must capture all inputs, outputs, commands run, command outputs, and agent inference.
Daemon configuration should be changed through the meta-signal socket; configuration verbs live in the meta-signal contract.

The 64-bit signal root-verb namespace is per-component, not workspace-wide: byte 0 is each channel's own root-verb enum (up to 256 verbs), keyed for decoding by provenance (which channel/socket the log came from), while universal data variants (U8, U16) stay shared in bytes 1-7. The namespace is partitioned into a SignalCore system-types zone and a component-specific zone with pre-allocated sizes; repartitioning is a major-version event. Byte 0 may split by golden ratio (~0.39/0.61) between owner and public contracts (default owner-small, public-big), enforced at compile time (both contracts must agree or compilation fails), opening a single-socket-per-component design where byte 0 encodes the namespace.

domain-criome is the authority for .criome domains — a content-addressed DNS where each domain (e.g. goldragon.criome) is its own content-addressable authority server queried via its daemon, last-snapshot as cached state. A daemon may host multiple domains and resolve ones it doesn't own from agreed last-known cached records; domain-criome decides node ownership, with NotAuthoritative(Delegation) redirect as the off-network fallback.
Spirit archive storage should have a sensible daemon-derived default path and be configurable through daemon configuration.
System-operator is an operator lane for implementation when the psyche explicitly directs it to work production code, not only a deploy-or-report lane.

Micro-macros are small simple composable macro units that can be named in specific positions within larger macros. Examples: enum-short-syntax-applier, struct-short-syntax-applier, structure-match dispatcher. Larger macros compose them by calling them at attachment points - a macro library pattern where macros are reusable building blocks rather than monolithic transformations.

Every component repository gets a concept schema file starting at version 0.1; all components need schemas, with Spirit, Orchestrate, Mind, and Persona as preferred pilots. Schema files create the data types for the libraries they serve, remain the source of data-type truth, and may live beside Rust source when simplest. Schema files can be split by contract or schema-file type so parsing is per-file and positional over root NOTA objects rather than one overloaded monolith.

Sema gets its own short header symmetric with signal-side. The sema-engine operations (executor, lowering, command dispatch, database reads/writes) ALL carry the same 8-enum 64-bit short-header structure, with sema-specific root + sub-enum vocabulary. Tap-anywhere observability extends to sema-side operations identically to signal-side. Two parallel headers — signal-header and sema-header — using the same macro machinery but routing to different paths. This is the symmetric counterpart to spirit 388: short headers are universal across both the wire (signal) and the engine (sema) surfaces.

Each launched role/agent receives a short identifier derived from the first three bytes of its Criome master-identity public key, exposed via a shortest_id() method on the full ID that returns the shortest non-colliding prefix (3 bytes default, widening up to the NOTA namespace width on collision). Because identities are binary in the signal layer, the collision check is a direct byte comparison on the public-key prefix.
Contract schemas should be explicit content-addressable layout schemas for rkyv migration

Intent agglomeration and refresh is triggered by an automated auditor that auto-proposes refreshes; the psyche confirms the retire of source records. Automated discovery with human-gated removal, preserving the conservative over-removal-is-worse discipline.

Splitting SEMA out of the daemon is only a distant-future consideration if the database must become larger and more available; it is not the current per-plane-crate design and should not be emphasized in a way that confuses agents.

Pi harness aborts around compaction are a recurring reliability problem, not an isolated one-off; future investigations should treat similar stop-after-compaction symptoms as harness bug candidates unless user/manual abort is confirmed. *debugging*

Cross-system trust root for criome: a cluster-root identity signs member keys, and that signature is criome registry admission gate (RegisterIdentity requires a valid root signature); cross-system peers trust keys chained to the cluster root. This closes criome current no-admission-control gap (the registry accepts any key today) and gives a non-circular first-cross-system-trust bootstrap.
Versioned CLIs need an active-version selector. Spirit exposes version lookup as the bare NOTA input Version (spirit Version) — not a Unix flag and not a parenthesized empty record.

Nexus has an internal interface with a second engine inside it that is recursive — it can call back on itself. This inner engine is the workspace meta/runtime control layer: it handles agent/actor prioritization (when Nexus actors should run more than SEMA actors because database is busy or Nexus needs to keep processing); backpressure (Nexus notifies clients the database is too busy when overloaded); runtime scheduling decisions about which actors run when. Outer NexusEngine handles per-request domain decisions; inner runtime engine handles meta-decisions about the runtime itself. Future direction enabling deeper control over the actor system when overloaded — Nexus stays available to take organizing decisions even under load. *resource management*

If a separate weight axis is added, it uses the same qualitative Magnitude ladder (Zero through Maximum) on a second axis distinct from certainty — not an integer count — keeping the contract all-qualitative; weight would preserve accumulated importance across agglomeration after source mentions vanish. Low-certainty design: production needs no immediate weight migration; weight stays in future record-shape design until the mechanism settles.

lojix integrates with criome-based authentication as part of production cutover: the lojix deploy daemon's operational credentials and unattended machine identity are custodied and authenticated through criome rather than borrowing the operator's logged-in session (GPG/SSH agent). Builds on agents holding cryptographic identity via criome.
Spirit releases carry schema-aware version numbers, and deployed Spirit versions are tagged before schema-migration planning.

Spirit should gain a ChangePrivacy operation mirroring ChangeCertainty — change an existing record privacy Magnitude in place, preserving identifier and daemon timestamp, instead of requiring remove plus re-record. Motivated by privacy elevation of existing intent records.
Schema examples should include multi-variant headers, not only one-element vectors.

Harness production shape should be one component daemon owning multiple harness instances internally; one OS daemon per harness is too heavy for the intended design. Per-harness boundaries live as records/actors/adapters inside the harness daemon, not as separate component processes.

Manifest the recurring architecture patterns across active worktrees, primary skills, and workspace intent: async mail flow, mail-system actor objects, object-oriented Rust methods on schema-generated types, and related repeated implementation disciplines should be durable instructions rather than only report-local notes.

On next-version failure (catastrophic or partial-support), main recovers caller intent: it reconstructs what it can from the original message via partial application and records the divergence, treating partial support the same as cant-do-at-all to preserve caller intent across version divergence.

Roles in persona-orchestrate's lane registry are NOTA vectors of identifier tokens (e.g. [PersonaSignal Designer]). The LAST token is the base discipline (authority chain + skill loading); preceding tokens are specializations. Filesystem form is the hyphen-joined lowercase rendering ([Note Designer] becomes note-designer). Tokens are open identifiers; ordinal prefixes (second-, third-) only disambiguate same-role lanes.

Cluster configuration and Horizon should carry the public-domain mapping for ordinary DNS fallback: criome.net is assigned per cluster, with goldragon owning goldragon.criome.net; the exact NOTA shape is open, but the data belongs in cluster config rather than being hardcoded downstream.

When a designer creates a concept on a concept branch, the designer files a bead for the operator to review and integrate it, noting the most important things — making concept work visible and actionable rather than relying on chat the operator may never see.
Trace client behavior belongs in a reusable client library that can enable NOTA display or SEMA-backed trace logging; the CLI should be a thin wrapper around that library.

Content-extracting/emitting Spirit operations accept a customizable output-target enum as the final request field — variants Stdout, Stderr, File(path). Stderr is a normal output option, not an error channel. CollectRemovalCandidates uses it; future export/dump ops the same way, keeping the wire interface uniform.

Agents should surface dependency relationships routinely — who depends on what, what each repo/component uses and is used by — especially in engine audits and thorough reports, so the psyche keeps a running sense of what is used by what across many repositories.

LLM calls are computation units configured as a default provider plus an ordered fallback-provider chain tried in sequence, with per-provider config tweaks evaluated at the typed daemon boundary. The daemon library is the reducer plus checker, including authorization that can escalate to Criome — giving failure-tolerance and provider portability.

Assembled schema reserves scalar pass-through type references for built-in scalars (String, U64, Boolean and similar Rust primitive/domain-floor types) so the assembled form writes the scalar directly instead of wrapping it as Plain(String). Those reserved names are unavailable for user-defined data types.
Dual-version upgrade replies must distinguish old-database and new-database write failures.

CriomOS communication has two transport lanes: Router is the ordinary fabric for cross-sandbox/cross-network traffic (router-to-router remote delivery, reference push, payload-blind byte movement), and criome has a direct criome-to-criome peer lane for time-sensitive agreement (quorum signing, crystallized-time windows) carrying only agreement/authorization messages — keeping criome auth-only and Router general transport. Each agent runs in its own microVM sandbox; the tailnet gives confidentiality and criome BLS attestations per-frame authenticity; intra-host sandbox-to-router transport is tap/L3 (vsock deferred for losing SO_PEERCRED). Mirror version-controls and moves objects.

Spirit keeps separate ordinary and owner sockets for now, permissions enforced by filesystem socket access under trusted local development (a future general socket with explicit permission prefix is possible but not the current target). Parallel daemon versions need versioned sockets. The multi-version dispatch protocol routes the CLI to the next-version daemon, which coordinates back with main; a missing-next returns an error logged as failed. Upgrade orders triggering the smart handover arrive through the target component's owner socket. PeerCheck is retired — signal-version-handover is the single discovery mechanism for next-version daemons.

Header declarations come first and drive receive/dispatch triage, representing the outermost shape. Headers should be derivable from the actual assembled data type structure rather than authored in a separate place. Schema entry points usually begin with an enum-like variant decision because the first structural match creates the routing namespace, mirroring the binary signal header. Schema creates base Input and Output enums for actor reaction types; execution logic matches these variants to choose reply and state action.
Cloud is the home for provider API machinery (Cloudflare, Google, cloud hosters such as Hetzner); cloud plan preparation belongs on the owner signal surface.
Closing duplicate beads must preserve all information from the closed bead; competing design ideas are kept so agents in those fields can compare and essay them

The schema for Signal/Nexus/Sema planes is the REACTIVE SCHEMA, an extension of a simpler BASE SCHEMA (root struct plus namespace of type definitions, pure data specification). Plain schema says what data IS; reactive schema adds the reaction surface (input in, nexus through, sema state, output back) and says how data MOVES — analogous to Nix's built-in derivation extended by higher-level builders. Most schemas are plain; a component top-level schema is reactive. The root schema must itself be described by an explicit schema object — the .schema reader may know it reads the root Schema type, but Schema still needs a concrete schema of its own fields. Syntax details (namespace ordering, rooting, field-name derivation) remain open.

Testing and deployment are one function in the lojix deploy component: both build an OS/cluster closure and bring it up on a target, differing only in containment. Triad exposes both faces — ordinary non-meta signal targets contained throwaway resources (hermetic VMs, sandboxes, on-demand VmHost guests, ephemeral cloud droplets it provisions and reaps) so a broken run kills only the contained target; meta signal is privileged production deploy to real nodes. The ordinary-vs-meta split is the safety boundary, enforced by typed contained-vs-production targets, not a runtime flag.
For desktop/resource-safety work, research the necessary components and mechanisms first, then implement the smallest safe shape that preserves the active session — no shortcut implementations.

Psyche-report is a distinct report kind: self-contained (explains in deep context, not quoting other artifacts), for both psyche and agents, detailed with extensive visuals (mermaid), staged through the process, focused on a single topic exploring its biggest decision. Begin with an Intent Anchors section listing the central bracket-quoted intent summaries, one blank line between, before analysis. Path under reports/psyche/.

Designer takes leans on open psyche questions by extrapolating from observed psyche patterns, presents them in writing with uncertainties named, and the psyche reviews via Git in a later session — keeping the workspace moving when the psyche is away.

Spirit guardian admits intent captures whose operative guidance states the affirmative shape to follow. Captures framed primarily as exclusion prohibition or definition by negation are returned to the submitting agent for positive rewording before admission.

Public-traffic routing uses Persona-orchestrated FD handoff via SCM_RIGHTS (Design D) for atomic lossless cutover: clients connect to ONE stable public socket per component; Persona binds it, accepts connections, hands accepted FDs to the active-version daemon over a per-component control connection, then stays off the byte path. Same socket model in dev and prod.

Each Rust crate in schema generation carries a standard schema folder with lib.schema as entry point, loading module schemas (e.g. signal.schema) through that root in Rust module style. Schema imports resolve by crate name from that folder, so schema and source travel together as one compilation and namespace unit.

Under the current macro grammar the head symbols in ((Parse Expression) (Render Expression) ...) read as macro/operator heads, not enum variants, so this double-wrapped Input/Output form is denied as schema grammar until a distinct enum-variant context or explicit enum form is established.

Research a way for core interactive programs (desktop shell/compositor, terminal emulator, launcher, dedicated rescue terminal) to keep higher effective CPU/IO/memory priority than bulk work so the user stays in control under resource pressure; survey Linux approaches rather than assuming a mechanism. Favor only the specific UI components needed over broadly restarting desktop session slices; the rescue terminal is separate so it gets protected policy without boosting heavy agent/build children.

Full request pipeline: Signal triages wire input → Nexus computes → SEMA performs durable op → Nexus receives SEMA reply → Nexus decides next steps → Nexus returns reply to Signal → Signal frames reply to wire client. Asynchronous throughout; rolling origin identifiers thread through the whole pipeline so each layer routes responses back to the correct waiter, including partial SEMA responses for multi-op work. *IPC*
The backup WiFi password is a human-readable randomly generated secret stored through sops-nix rather than plaintext source or logs.
Intent capture should be denser and less verbose: durable records preserve the clarified intent without large verbatim blocks that bloat output and become lossy to work with.
Spirit identifier migration data should be stored as NOTA data; SEMA is for schema/engine structure, not for the migration data file itself.
For orchestrate release safety, the release invariant must be specified independently of bookmark names; a bookmark prefix is insufficient evidence of lane state.

criome owns key custody and is the authorization decider. A requesting component submits a content-addressed object to its local per-Unix-user criome - the submitter authenticated by SO_PEERCRED (2st7) - and criome, holding the keys (9s52), decides the verdict and signs it. The requester does not hold or use a request-signing key; authorization is criome's decision (auto-approve, client-approval via mentci, or a criome-node quorum per t00s), not a requester signature that criome merely verifies. This settles the contradiction between criome's architecture text and the mentci/9s52 key-store model in favor of criome-owns-the-key-store: criome's earlier wording that other identities hold their own keys and criome only verifies their signatures is corrected to match.

The recording system data flow goes from the psyche's laptop microphone, across the network, to the large-ai-node where the always-on capture buffer and real-time multi-modal inference live. The laptop is the audio origin; the large-ai-node is the processing host. The architecture in /1 (recording-system design) implicitly assumed the audio source and processor were the same machine; refine to make the laptop-to-large-ai-node hop explicit.

A .schema file reads in a known Schema context: its root struct type is implied by the extension/filename (like Rust modules), so fields appear positionally with no outer wrapper. The root holds a key-value map of datatype names to raw NOTA objects, first field the imports/exports namespace (brace map) then input/output struct(s) in square brackets, combinable. Reading needs both meta-schema and concrete schema through the same self-describing format; authored data files prefer typed NOTA whose filename defines the root type.
Persona-Pi acceptance includes live terminal usability and readable themes; the default Pi profile is the fully-featured basic experience, and Pi exposes both terminal-cell and harness API surfaces.

The root schema-generated signal object carries the signal-frame protocol behavior for rkyv serialization and process-to-process dispatch, integrating caller/process-origin support when that library is ready.

The fully-qualified-symbol-path mechanism should be highlighted explicitly in ESSENCE INTENT ARCHITECTURE files and schema-related skills. It is the workspace's universal machine-readable symbol identity expressed through a text-user-interface form. Reports and designs that use symbol identity should call out that this is the canonical naming and lookup mechanism, not invent per-design alternatives. Schema-emitted Rust types and NOTA renderings are two projections of one underlying symbol-path identity space. *interoperability*
Lane retirement requires context maintenance on leftover memories (reports + beads) before identifier retirement; context-maintenance discipline encompasses retired-lane sweeps

TraceEvent is a transparent newtype or direct ObjectName trace root when its only payload is the activated object name, yielding a single object shape rather than noisy double-delimited NOTA from a one-field struct.

Every ARCHITECTURE.md carries a Possible future design section holding deferred/possible features with enough framing to pick up later, so future direction is recorded in place. Restore (re-asserting archived records into the hot store, inverse of collect) is recorded there as a possible future feature, not current work.

The criome daemon holds an encrypted multi-key store: a master BLS keypair generated on first run, plus sub-keys. It always has a self-start key — the user home private key, the only one readable by the daemon/owning user, encrypted at rest with home-user or system encryption; other keys are sub-encrypted within the store. The secret never leaves criome; the owner can rotate on suspected leak. Transitional bootstrap until meta-signal-criome carries authenticated key config; hardware key mechanism is a later refinement.

Macro dispatch is two-phase: a structure-match macro first asks what shape the NotaValue is (enum short-syntax? struct? newtype?), then the matching per-shape transformation macro applies its lowering — they compose because the same syntactic placement can carry different shapes. Schema variants are ordinary vectors or macros, and each macro carries its own schema-reading logic, customizing interpretation by structural shape (e.g. curly-bracket with a single identifier; curly-bracket with even item count where all key positions resolve to a consistent type) rather than positional tokens. This is the schema language's extension mechanism; new shapes arrive as new macros enter the precompiled core or per-schema imports.

The horizon node model derives its facets from the typed source enums — NodeSpecies for the cluster role and MachineSpecies (Metal or Pod) for the substrate — computed directly. Role facets in BehavesAs, which are unions over several species and form the cross-repo CriomOS gating contract, are read from the typed NodeSpecies. A node's substrate is the already-typed MachineSpecies on machine.species, so it is a first-class typed value with no separate enum needed. Adding a node species adds no parallel one-hot bool struct.

The data-lifecycle ladder is a closed named set worth building — nominate, tombstone, archive, collect, compact, purge — rather than ad-hoc operations. The system designer should make a concept of it. It lands on the new schema-derived spirit, not the hand-written production daemon, since it is a large feature.

Report versioning: uncommitted reports edit in place freely; committed reports take minor corrections in place but major changes (reframing, recommendation reversal, large rewrite) get a versioned successor filename (NNN-v2-, v3-) preserving the prior in history. When context corrects a fresh report the agent still holds, edit it directly — action over narration. Document the v-versioning rule in the reporting skill.
Spirit timestamp is daemon-stamped
Schema macros compose from reusable micro-macros

The closed Domain taxonomy is the broad-routing layer only; fine-grained specificity belongs to the open referent layer of named particulars and to free-text description keywords for narrow concepts, not to deepening the closed enum. A taxonomy layer or leaf earns its place only when it disambiguates and carries real routing load; over-specified leaves that function as referents or keywords are cut. Applied now: the Technology Software subtree is coarsened to curated broad-field leaves under terminal-able sub-domains.

engine logic at the high level is tree-vs-tree matching; canonically enum-against-enum, or enum-against-a-mix-of-enums-and-method-calls; when two enums meet under match, the cross-product of their variants is a common-language relationship node point that should be made explicit (often via a trait) rather than scattered across ad-hoc match arms
Bird/Aether has her own forked workspace and soul repositories

Private intent capture gains an explicit named short-form (e.g. RecordPrivate/RecordSealed) that lowers to a normal Spirit record with elevated privacy Magnitude, making private capture one deliberate unmissable ritual; ordinary record shorthands default to open public privacy Zero.
Spirit record acceptance replies should display the shortest collision-free lowercase identifier prefix with a minimum length of four characters, not the full stored identifier.

Schema has a sectioned three-part structure (parts 2-3 optionally recursive): (1) SPECIFYING — imports and exports / name-sharing; (2) INPUT — operations and queries received; (3) OUTPUT — responses emitted. Each part's first sub-part is a header (or derived from the assembled schema). Per component the struct order is: optional metadata/imports, then three short headers (regular signal, owner signal, sema operation) each as a vector of variants, then namespace map, optionally a final extension vector. The top-level outer vector holds distinct typed sub-vector sections (e.g. messaging objects, then type namespace), not one flat mixed vector; roots are positional structs using square brackets since structs are ordered field vectors.
When an old-version message is upgraded and accepted, the runtime should emit an observable event so introspection, routers, or agents can notify the source to upgrade its client schema.

Multi-version daemon coexistence via version-suffixed names: persona daemons of different versions run side-by-side on one machine, sockets and CLI binaries named by version (e.g. spirit-v0.1.1), with a shim/alias resolving plain unsuffixed names (spirit) to the environment's current default version. Exact shim mechanism is system-specialist territory.
Browser automation for real user accounts should support attaching to a visible browser tab/session so the human can watch, intervene, and keep login/2FA secrets out of agent prompts and logs.

The bootstrap core schema (the schema-schema) is implemented in ordinary Rust first and exposed as the macro interface for writing schema macros: it names macro inputs by type (a macro's input type IS its name in the macro), so macros inspect Nota block structure and emit assembled schema data, and people build their own macros against this core. Every schema file is loaded with the schema-schema attached implicitly; it defines the structural shape expected for schema files (root struct + sections) and is the lowest-level macro primitive everything builds on. Built-in macros bootstrap expansion before the user-declared macro space (the schema's creator space for typed macros) expands.

The schema lowering engine producing AssembledSchema is built from reusable data-carrying macro-variant lowerers at node-definition points: each macro-schema variant carries a struct defining its input type, and at a node-definition the builtin engine dispatches by shape to the matching variant, which consumes its input struct to produce lowered output. The engine is extensible by adding new variants plus their input structs, not by hard-coding shape rules. SchemaMacro in authored macro-library source is a typed/tagged variant carrying the macro-definition payload (not an untyped sentinel string), collected by the macro-library container before projection to serialized macro-library data.

The resolved assembled-schema artifact is the Asschema (.asschema) file: pure NOTA holding fully resolved enum and struct definitions with no remaining macros, read against the known Asschema root struct positionally with no outer wrapper. Human-readable debug output is not .asschema and must be named as a witness.
Ask fewer questions at a time, with enough context to answer directly

Spirit observation should support intelligent topic retrieval emphasizing recent intent by default while adapting historical depth to topic frequency — high-churn topics stay near the edge, quiet topics reach farther back for a comparable useful result set. Search may grow beyond simple filters toward weighted keyword or recency scoring, potentially using the Nexus language for richer query logic (exploratory direction, not a settled immediate requirement).

When debugging or rewriting a part of the system, actively evaluate the design of the parts being touched and surface any flaw or improvement opportunity to the psyche as you find it. Improving code is paired with judging its design, not just making it work; raise what you see rather than silently working around it.

Schema is a SUPERSET of Cap'n Proto-style spec languages, adding (1) its own module system, (2) a macro system, (3) shape-driven node-type matching (structure + member count + member types resolve a node's type) — a real spec language without becoming general-purpose. Scope: the workspace's Rust subset only, no tuples (tuples are poorly-defined structs). The recursive-parser + dispatch + lowering model is a reusable library for any NOTA DSL (Shen-style mid-compilation assembly); schema is its first application. The aski-core/askic pattern is an analog informing the discipline, not a foundation to replicate.

Schema inline declarations inside root input/output payloads stay shallow — inline bodies for operation-level shape, deeper reusable or nested structure moved into named namespace types rather than buried in root vectors. Inline declarations are promoted into the source type scope: later inline references (Topic *) resolve to an earlier inline Topic String, and library consumers see promoted types through the namespace/export surface without duplicate trailing namespace entries.

Intent gaps are filled by cross-pollinating patterns found elsewhere in the intent — when one area of intent lacks a clear design, look for an analogous pattern already established in another area and project it onto the gap. Surfaces broken or implicit conventions and turns them into stated principles. Used during intent-clarity audits of recent work.

Intent refresh and agglomeration is primarily agent behavior trained through a skill, not engine logic: agents learn how to refresh many related intents into fewer stronger records. The only supporting code is the relations field; the refreshing judgment lives in agent behavior and the intent-maintenance skill.
Schema and upgrade work should carry explicit provenance.

Sema-upgrade is the universal stateful schema-upgrade component for sema databases (persona-component typed-record stores). It boots FIRST as engine-pre-zero infrastructure owned by the engine manager, before any persona daemon, and checks each daemon's stored schema-version-hash against the hash its code declares. The SEMA interface owns transitory dual-database concurrency during a live transition. First test case: treat the legacy intent log as a 0.01 spirit database; the schema-spec-language eventually drives transforms from declarative diffs.
STT may hear sema as sim; normalize to sema before storing verbatim

Component data lifecycle prefers explicit archival lowering before hard deletion: stale or low-certainty data is marked and moved out of the hot working DB to keep runtime state manageable, recoverability best-effort unless a stronger retention class applies. When a record moves to the archive its privacy variable moves with it, preserved at its original privacy level, and archive reads honor the same explicit privacy discipline as the live store.

Signal engine role is message triage only — admission, dispatch, identity-stamping, validation, wire-frame handling. Signal does not own heavy algorithmic logic or decision-making; it routes input to Nexus and routes Nexus replies back to wire output. *remote procedure call*

Spirit topics are user-creatable single strings — broad atomic single-word concepts, not compound hyphenated phrases (prefer two topics [intent logging] over [intent-log]); a multi-concept record uses multiple topic words. Reuse existing topic words when they cover the substance; invent new ones only when none fits, to keep records discoverable by either concept and avoid near-synonym vocabulary explosion. Spirit entries carry one clarified description and no verbatim field.
Bird/Zeus update authority uses LiGoldragon main by default, not per-user branches

The schema root plane surface is a data-carrying enum named Plane with Signal, Nexus, and Sema variants carrying the actual plane messages, so runtime code matches directly on the plane rather than pairing a thin kind tag with a separate envelope. This supersedes the earlier thin per-plane envelope-type approach while keeping its goal: engine trait signatures type-check on plane membership (a signal-plane message cannot pass where a nexus/sema message is expected), so the trait chain is impossible to mis-wire on order and plane axes.
The current target assumes at most seven root data-carrying variants for a component surface; if a component needs substantially more, that is pressure to split the component or surface.

Everything reading NOTA-shaped structure above the raw parser goes through typed structural macro nodes; surviving hand-parsing sites (e.g. the schema-next macro library) are violations to fix, and a shape structural macro nodes cannot express is surfaced to the psyche, not worked around. NOTA itself should have a schema, its generated Rust schema-derived rather than a hand-maintained parser island. Dispatch precedence among competing reference forms (built-in heads, then declared macros, then the generic catch-all) is reified as an explicit typed NOTA value, so every reader and the resolver consult one canonical declared precedence.

lojix carries logic for a comfortable, easy-to-use public test-authoring interface: schema shorthands and well-developed option setting and querying, so authoring a cluster test such as a criome, spirit, and router cluster test on lojix's ordinary contained-testing interface is ergonomic rather than verbose. Ease of use is a first-class requirement of the interface, not an afterthought.

Square brackets are NOTA's raw vector container delimiter, not the schema syntax for declaring a Vec; declare a vector type with an explicit parenthesized typed object (Vec of X). At a typed position expecting String or a string newtype, bracket content reads as string data (a string is a vector of characters), so [] is not always a vector.
Short identifiers should be derived as methods on full identifiers with collision checks

Multi-argument type references and applications use NOTA's flat positional form — head then arguments inline, like Map then K then V — not a grouped form that wraps the arguments in their own delimiters. The head already names the construct, so a trained human or LLM reads the positional arguments correctly without extra grouping, and the flat form is fewer characters while staying equally legible. This settles the Map type-reference shape as the flat head-K-V form and generalizes to multi-argument type-reference applications.
Per-repo data-type discipline (signal-X repos with versioned schema libraries) is what enables cross-version decoding and recovery
Development Nix inputs may point at feature branch names for mutable role lanes

Role-token-driven skill loading: each token in a lane's role vector contributes to the default skill bundle the persona-mind sends at boot. A lane with role [PersonaSignal Designer] loads the base designer skills (skills/designer.md) plus persona-signal-specific skills; [Note Designer] loads designer skills plus note-specific. Specialization tokens map directly to skill files (or skill bundles registered for that token). Composes with the existing intent that skills bundle into roles (intent/persona.nota 2026-05-20T17:30); refines it by making the bundle composition derivable from the role vector rather than from a single role string.

mentci-egui socket displays identify the connected component and authority channel, such as Criome and MetaCriome, rather than generic Ordinary and Meta labels, because Mentci will connect to multiple component sockets.

When a match between two domains has no defined behavior in a cell, the response is a typed error or help message (Unavailable / Unauthorized / NotImplemented / etc.) that lives in the domain enum as part of the trait surface, so the caller can match on it — not an exception or unhandled default.

mentci-egui is the interactive GUI client for the mentci daemon: it connects to the daemon over the signal contracts, offers ordinary operation mode plus a root-like meta mode for privileged meta-signal operations, renders typed replies and unknown inner objects through a NOTA text fallback until purpose-built GUI views exist, and is shaped for long-lived daemon subscriptions and incoming events rather than the single synchronous request-reply feel of the CLI.

The intent recorder uses voice recognition as the cutoff between recorded-and-processed and ignored audio: it ignores audio not recognised as the psyche by default (filtering out the psyche's wife who shares the residence). If the active microphone passes to the wife, the recorder recognises the voice change and routes her speech to her own dynamically-spawned persona instance — captured intent belongs to her as a distinct psyche with her own intent layer.
Spirit should support agent catch-up queries from a recorded time so agents can retrieve intent added since their last read without depending on numeric identifier order.

CriomOS gains a node feature for VM-based testing using the best Linux VM tech: a real display/GPU/DRM-capable VM (headless wlroots lacks wlr-gamma-control), Nix-native, CI-automatable and interactively viewable, to test CriomOS and visual/UI/GPU components end-to-end — launch the real app, run the component, observe the actual visual effect (theme/warmth/brightness) while watching for freezes and errors. chroma is the first target; sandboxes can run via systemd on a node such as Prometheus.

Macro loading uses a first indexing pass that collects all macro names from the schema and its imports before any macro is invoked; later passes resolve references by name from the index, enabling forward and out-of-order references across imports, with lazy loading (a macro loads only when referenced during dispatch). Expansion needs indexed reading order with lazy resolution, and passes may hand generic NOTA subobjects to later passes. Macro handlers receive NotaBody streams with outer wrapper delimiters stripped at match time, not the wrapper.

For a prompt addressing multiple lanes, the default capturer is the first responder — in practice the operator (Codex), faster than the designer (Claude) — who writes the Spirit entry; the slower lane then gap-checks and gap-fills only a genuine omission rather than writing a parallel entry. Per psyche 2026-06-05.
Code should be elegant succinct and self-describing *maintainability*

Authored-schema declaration forms: omit only the root object name (reader knows root type); every nested object names itself. Enum = (qualified-name, paren-of-qualified-symbols), must name its type (bare variant list invalid); struct = bracket second-object form; one-symbol bracket = newtype. Root struct fields are bare; Name@Delimiter applies only to user declarations of new type names — additional plane roots (NexusInput/Output, SemaInput/Output) keep Name@. Inline PascalCase before references = local reusable types; camelCase = field names. Pipe = generics/traits; assembled schema stays legal NOTA. Spirit schema files: no outer root parens, header/root first, enums as ordered variant vectors, cover ordinary/owner/Sema headers.

schema-next needs a Bytes primitive (ideally fixed-size byte arrays) beyond String/Integer/Boolean/Path, so binary values (BLS keys, signatures, digests, fingerprints, nonces) type as bytes. It also needs a hash-identifier type whose value is bytes but whose NOTA projection is a canonical short string code, generalizing the 12-byte base36 RecordIdentifier.

The persona-pi extension adapts Pi APIs into the workspace signal-data-tree philosophy rather than mirroring Pi's flat namespace: methods belong to nouns, typed records over flat functions, recursive enum-shaped namespaces (a flat name like query_x_from_behind becomes an object with from_front/from_behind methods). Underlying Pi machinery stays Pi-shaped.

When reconciling the asschema roundtrip design, prefer simple canonical NOTA syntax and separated typed-object responsibilities: Asschema owns assembled schema data, AsschemaArtifact owns NOTA and rkyv artifact projection, a SEMA store owns durable rkyv persistence, and Rust emission consumes the typed Asschema object rather than parsing side syntax.

Create a standard engine situation report discipline that summarizes components, production code size, schema size, generated Rust size, interfaces, signatures, and graphs so agents and the psyche can quickly understand a system.

Universal data variants (U8, U16, possibly more primitives) are pre-allocated across all signal namespaces; every namespace inherits them as primitive sub-variants. Uses: Criome's 16-bit short ID for its public key (human-readable in logs), generic counters, small fixed-size values shared across components.

Criome authorization contracts should be content-addressed composable objects: each component can decide acceptance based on another accepted object or contract, and those dependencies should refer to each other's content-addressed contract objects rather than ad hoc mutable state. Threshold, majority, or time-window acceptance policies are criome contract logic layered on those composable authorization objects.
insert-only proof

the skill currently called autonomous-agent gets renamed and refocused as human-interaction; it covers the rules for how agents interact with the psyche specifically, since that interaction is what these rules are actually about
CriomOS must build Spirit's binary startup archive during Nix build/evaluation so the user service starts instantly instead of generating the archive at service startup or activation.

Cloud compute nodes are provisioned only once they have a concrete cluster role — provision-on-role, not standing speculative droplets. doris stays a declared, low-trust cluster member with a clearly-marked placeholder host key and remains unprovisioned until it is given a role; only then is an actual droplet provisioned and its real host key wired into the cluster data.

The assembled-schema visibility wrapper is a normal NOTA data-carrying variant: (Public Name Value) and (Private Name Value), with Public/Private as the variant head followed by name and value fields.
Primary should have a gitignored top-level private-repos directory for private repositories, separate from the normal repos symlink index.

Per-role model selection — agent roles map to the best-fit LLM models. Claude is the designer model (architecture, structure, fit). Codex is the advisor model (review, falsification, working-against-the-grain). GPT-latest (with Maximum Thinking enabled) is the Persona Pi default. The role determines which model the orchestrator selects; composite role versions are possible (one role can dispatch to multiple models in parallel and select / merge). The model-to-role mapping is a first-class orchestration decision, not a per-call detail.

A daemon may expose more than one signal protocol or signal interface. Configuration can be modeled as another typed signal surface, differentiated inside the root enumerator that contains the daemon's accepted signal surfaces.

Agents must not respond to Spirit overcapture by avoiding Spirit entirely; the correct discipline is to apply the conservative capture gate and use Spirit only for explicit durable decisions, principles, corrections, clarifications, and constraints.

Real-world end-to-end testing uses the most real production topology; sandbox shortcuts that omit a load-bearing piece are not e2e. For the message-passing engine, true e2e is a real agent sending a real message to another real agent whose response returns to the first; a routed daemon path ending at a fake terminal or acceptance socket is only a routing-harness witness, not full e2e.
Real-time streaming is a new Signal capability (working name signal-real-time): it is Signal, not Sema, and its storage format is open (likely a native real-time format rather than Sema).
Guardian training starts as a decision-log flywheel: every gated decision records candidate, retrieval bundle, verdict, and later override labels before any fine-tune. *model training*

Plain square brackets remain the desired candidate for inline string literals; the [|...|] block form is too much delimiter for common inline strings and is reserved for the multiline/bracket-safe case.

CriomOS provides a website-hosting node service so the psyche can host websites: a user configures a source to be rendered and served from a node, the service supports multiple renderer variants, and the standard default for now is a markdown-based static site in the Jekyll mould. The psyche requires the implementation be the most reliable and secure way to do it. This is the first concrete role doris, the low-trust DigitalOcean cloud node, can fill.

Long-term intent-recording direction: spirit-CLI-driven intent-recording-of-role for all roles, in real-time, with Prometheus running continuous capture; the chat-mediated capture pattern (psyche prompt → agent extracts → spirit-CLI record) gives way over time to direct real-time recording across every role's interaction surface.

Resolve the enum-vs-macro-invocation ambiguity on paren heads with an explicit discriminator: a collection macro (e.g. Vec on one element type) and a two-variant enum are structurally identical parens at the same node position, so delimiter-shape/position cannot distinguish operator-head from variant-name. Only the paren is doubly loaded; struct bracket and map brace are unambiguous. Candidate discriminators: reserved operator-word set (implicit), a sigil on operator heads (explicit), or moving collections off the paren onto suffix sigils + brace (vector=star suffix, option=question suffix, map=brace) leaving paren pure enum. Open: which discriminator, and whether enum stays the bare-paren default.

Code comments use NOTA-formatted signal records (NOTA-in-comments): markdown-readable and NOTA-syntax-highlightable, letting Mind read code-as-signal directly. Comments carry the why of each edit so the system can audit decisions and propose alternative designs from accumulated whys.

Component code-generation (designed/prototyped, not integrated): schema generates a component's Rust by expanding bound universal frames into concrete owned interfaces, not persisting generic aliases. Scope is narrow — repeated interface boilerplate: Input/Output and work/action enums, payload structs/newtypes/enums, standard inherent/trait impls (constructors, accessors, From legs, scalar-newtype Display/AsRef/comparisons), role/marker trait impls, opt-in mechanical Deref for the newtype shape, later VariantMatch for enum-rewrap. Traits/impls are the pipe-brace construct expressing Rust relationships as data so hand-written engine code accepts generated nouns via typed bounds. Method bodies are data only for the small mechanical family the schema shape uniquely proves (newtype Deref projection); business logic stays hand-written. Principle: a schema-authored body is valid only when every call resolves from the receiver's schema shape. Open: generic impl-header emission with parameter-bound vocabulary, fn/signature sub-construct for trait declarations, ownership-aware payload-vs-into_payload projection, total VariantMatch; settled constraints and open how live in schema-next/nota-next architecture files.

Open macro-node design frontiers, represented as macro-node configuration/data rather than parser magic: macro bodies need an explicit binding-and-reference mechanism for assigned symbols (a sigil such as dollar-sign is a candidate, the firm constraint being references stay visible in the schema language); and single-object sigil sugar where a leading sigil makes one NOTA object stand for a larger pair or field expression when the sigil makes the intended macro case obvious.

Refines designer 476 per operator's correction. Nexus has a schema-defined decision/effect language for non-SEMA decisions — typed NexusDecision wraps Signal SemaWrite SemaRead Effect; typed NexusEffect carries side-channel variants like Stash Fanout Summarize Drop Enqueue Preempt Cascade. Generated runner loops — Nexus decides; runner executes SEMA and effect actions; completions re-enter Nexus; only NexusDecision::Signal(Output) exits to wire. Side effects are typed without pretending every side effect is already a Signal reply. The LANGUAGE existence is Maximum; the exact variant list and implementation cost remain designer-side proposals at lower magnitude pending evidence.

Cloud credential custody moves toward system-level credentials: the current wire-supplied CredentialHandle the cloud daemon resolves to a process env var behind the 0o600 owner socket is the accepted transitional shape; the eventual direction is system-custodied machine credentials following the criome-custodied machine-identity pattern rather than a wire-selected env var name.

Each agent role gets its own workspace — a sub-workspace that is its own Git repository (called role-space, even though psyche acknowledges the name is awkward). Write access is gated via Git push, which is gated on the agent's SSH key (set when the agent starts). Each role has its own research lane within this space. This is the substrate that backs per-role isolation: designer-space, operator-space, poet-space, etc., each a separate repository whose push permissions are bound to whichever agent is currently in that role. (Designing-mode speculation — much of this is not yet implemented; intent recorded so the design direction is captured.)

For the lojix cutover, substituter resolution from node name to Yggdrasil cache URL and public key moves into the daemon (the daemon gains horizon-read for substituters; the wire reverts to carrying bare node names instead of pre-resolved url/public-key pairs). This is a provisional for-now choice and the code must be marked must-be-replaced-by-better-design.
active report lanes should stay well below twelve

For the new schema stack, creating new repositories with clean names is an acceptable option if branch names and old repository ancestry become too confusing. This is an option to evaluate, not an immediate rename directive.
Firmware gating reuses existing policy surfaces rather than adding broad Horizon schema.

Schema macro resolution maps nodes onto a closed macro space whose variants are the built-in type forms (Enum, Struct, Vec, Option, KeyValue, Newtype) via dual dispatch: look at the head keyword first, and if it is a reserved built-in (Vec, Option, KeyValue) dispatch directly and skip structural mapping; otherwise the node is a user type declaration resolved by structural shape — parenthesis holding an inner parenthesis is an enum, a bracket is a struct or newtype. In a strict brace key-value context a key-value pair is one semantic macro-node preserving the NOTA brace: qualified key plus brace value matches the struct macro, plus bracket value the enum macro, plus atom/reference value the newtype macro. Adds a keyword fast-path over the structure-and-position MacroRegistry.

Spirit may expose common search paths as explicit low-level shorthand Signal verbs, with public text lookup named PublicTextSearch rather than forcing agents through the full eight-field Observe query for ordinary public search.
Plural record replies (e.g. RecordsObserved) expose the vector directly in the signal tree rather than wrapping it in a single-field record that nests delimiters before the vector.

Bracket semantics: () is enum/variants, [] is struct/fields — a struct is a positional vector of fields ([] vector semantics), an enum is one-of alternatives (() grouping). Reading: Entry [Topic Kind Summary] is a struct; Kind (Decision Principle Correction) is an enum. Container forms like (Vec T) stay on () as a single-tagged-variant form (or [Vec T] under stricter reading — open).

The schema type — which of the three planes (Sema, Nexus, Signal/Data) a schema declares — is a Kind enum schema::Kind, each variant carrying that plane's schema body (the four-position content), so a schema document's root is a Kind value. This gives the three typed engines their dispatch point and mirrors the Sema runtime envelope at the message level. Kind-versus-Plane naming still open.
Signal Core can still exist as a concept; the prior rename moved content out of Signal Core, not the namespace itself
